DIA_PTI.name: Pork Tapeworm
DIA_PTI.description: Pork tapeworms (T. solium) are parasitic flatworms living in the digestive system of humans. Pigs act as intermediate hosts when they consume fodder or water contaminated with human feces. Eating undercooked pork can cause infection.

DIA_BTI.name: Beef Tapeworm
DIA_BTI.description: Beef tapeworms (T. saginata) are parasitic flatworms living in the digestive system of humans. Cattle act as intermediate hosts when they consume fodder or water contaminated with human feces. Eating undercooked beef can cause infection.

DIA_CKX.name: Chickenpox
DIA_CKX.description: Chickenpox, also known as varicella, is a highly contagious disease caused by the initial infection with Varicella zoster virus. The disease results in a characteristic skin rash that forms small, itchy blisters, which eventually scab over.

DIA_HRZ.name: Shingles
DIA_HRZ.description: Shingles is an infection caused by a reactivation of varicella-zoster virus. The disease results in a red skin rash that forms small blisters in a wide stripe following the course of a dermatome. Shingles can be extremely painful.

DIA_ONM.name: Nail Fungus
DIA_ONM.description: Nail fungus, also called Onychomycosis or Tinea unguium, is a fungal infection that affects fingers or toenails.

DIA_ATF.name: Athlete’s Foot
DIA_ATF.description: Athlete’s foot, also called Tinea pedis, is a highly contagious skin infection of the feet caused by fungi.

DIA_IDA.name: Iron-Deficiency Anemia
DIA_IDA.description: Anemia is defined as a reduced ability of the blood to carry oxygen. This can be caused by a decrease in red blood cells or hemoglobin. Iron-deficiency anemia is the most common form of anemia.

DIA_LIN.name: Lactose Intolerance
DIA_LIN.description: Lactose intolerance is the inability to digest lactose, a sugar found in milk products. Undigested lactose can result in bloating, diarrhea, gas or abdominal pain.

DIA_CLD.name: Common Cold
DIA_CLD.description: The common cold is a viral infection located in the upper respiratory system, usually the nose or throat.

DIA_ASN.name: Sinusitis
DIA_ASN.description: Sinusitis refers to inflammation of one or more sinuses. Sinusitis causes swelling and can compromise drainage of mucus or pus from the involved sinus cavity.

DIA_APH.name: Pharyngitis
DIA_APH.description: Pharyngitis refers to inflammation of the throat and can be caused by bacteria or virus.

DIA_ANG.name: Bacterial Tonsillitis
DIA_ANG.description: Bacterial tonsillitis is an infection of the tonsils. The most common bacterial tonsillitis is caused by Streptococcus pyogenes, and is known as 'strep throat'.

DIA_ANV.name: Viral Tonsillitis
DIA_ANV.description: Viral tonsillitis is an infection of the tonsils and can be caused by several viral agents.

DIA_LAR.name: Laryngitis
DIA_LAR.description: Laryngitis refers to inflammation of the voice box. It can be caused by bacteria or virus, but also because of excessive voice use.

DIA_BRN.name: Bronchitis
DIA_BRN.description: Bronchitis is an inflammation of the lower airways called bronchi and is usually caused by virus.

DIA_HFV.name: Hay Fever
DIA_HFV.description: Hay fever, medically known as allergic rhinitis, is an inflammation in the nose caused by an allergic reaction to an allergen in the air, such as pollen.

DIA_ACD.name: Contact Dermatitis
DIA_ACD.description: Contact dermatitis is a localized rash or irritation of the skin caused by contact to an allergen or irritant.

DIA_GEW.name: Golfer's Elbow
DIA_GEW.description: Golfer's elbow, also known as medial epicondylitis, is characterized by radiating pain from the inside of the elbow to the wrist. The condition is caused by performing repetitive movements resulting in irritation of the muscles and tendons responsible for said movements.

DIA_SCS.name: Scoliosis
DIA_SCS.description: Scoliosis is a sideway curvature of the spine usually in a C or S shape. Scoliosis is usually painless.

DIA_RYS.name: Raynaud's Syndrome
DIA_RYS.description: Raynaud's syndrome causes numbness and coldness of hands, fingers and toes as response to cold outdoor temperatures.

DIA_CFS.name: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
DIA_CFS.description: Chronic fatigue syndrome is very complicated disease that refers to a condition of fatigue that can not be explained by any underlying medical condition.

DIA_INS.name: Transient Insomnia
DIA_INS.description: Insomnia is defined as a subjective experience of bad sleep. This can be due to the inability to fall asleep, sleep continuously throughout the night or waking up too early.

TRM_ACT.name: Arm Contusion
TRM_ACT.description: A contusion, also known as a bruise, is a region of injured tissue usually with purple discoloration.

TRM_HCT.name: Hand Contusion
TRM_HCT.description: A contusion, also known as a bruise, is a region of injured tissue usually with purple discoloration.

TRM_FCT.name: Foot Contusion
TRM_FCT.description: A contusion, also known as a bruise, is a region of injured tissue usually with purple discoloration.

TRM_ACN.name: Ankle Contusion
TRM_ACN.description: A contusion, also known as a bruise, is a region of injured tissue usually with purple discoloration.

TRM_LCT.name: Leg Contusion
TRM_LCT.description: A contusion, also known as a bruise, is a region of injured tissue usually with purple discoloration.

TRM_CCT.name: Chest Contusion
TRM_CCT.description: A contusion, also known as a bruise, is a region of injured tissue usually with purple discoloration.

DIA_046.name: Influenza B
DIA_046.description: Influenza B is caused by the Influenza B virus, responsible for seasonal outbreaks of Influenza infection in humans.

DIA_SLM.name: Salmonellosis
DIA_SLM.description: Salmonella infection is one one of the common bacterial infection that affects the human intestinal tract. Humans become infected by ingesting contaminated water or food.

DIA_AMB.name: Amoebiasis
DIA_AMB.description: Amoebiasis is caused by Entamoeba histolytica, the infection spreads through water or food contaminated by feces.

DIA_GRD.name: Giardiasis
DIA_GRD.description: Giardia infection is one of the most common waterborne intestinal infections. Giardias can be transmitted through direct contact with infected individuals or by ingesting contaminated food.

DIA_PMI.name: Pulmonary NTMB Infection
DIA_PMI.description: Pulmonary mycobacterial infection of non-tuberculous type of disease caused by other species of mycobacterium and it is relatively common. Often occuring in immunoincompetent patients or in patients with preexisting pulmonary disease, but also in healthy patients.

DIA_IMN.name: Infectious Mononucleosis
DIA_IMN.description: Infectious mononucleosis is usually caused by EBV - Epstein-Barr Virus - people usually get infected in their childhood. There is no vaccine for the disease. In the 1920's it was described as "the kissing disease".

DIA_HPM.name: Histoplasmosis
DIA_HPM.description: Histoplasmosis also called Cave Disease is respiratory disease caused by the fungus Histoplasma Capsulatum that mainly affects the lungs. Symptoms vary greatly. Past infection creates partial immunity.

DIA_ASP.name: Aspergillosis
DIA_ASP.description: Aspergillosis is a respiratory disease caused by the fungus Aspergillus. If the patient has a weakened immune system, the invasive form of the disease spreads through the lungs. Infected lungs must undergo pulmonary surgery to clear the infection.

DIA_HLA.name: Hemolytic Anemia
DIA_HLA.description: Hemolytic anemia happens due to hemolysis, an abnormal breakdown of red blood cells in the blood vessels or elsewhere in the human body.

DIA_TCP.name: Thrombocytopenia
DIA_TCP.description: Thrombocytopenia represents a condition with significantly lower level of blood platelets, that are responsible for stopping bleeding by clumping and forming clots.

DIA_HPL.name: Hemophilia
DIA_HPL.description: Hemophilia is an inherited disease that causes low levels of clotting factors. Without sufficient levels of clotting factors, the blood does not clot properly which leads to excessive bleeding.

DIA_THL.name: Thalassaemia
DIA_THL.description: Thalassaemia is an inherited blood disorder with the formation of abnormal hemoglobin that binds oxygen less efficiently and makes the red blood cell out of shape.

DIA_SRCL.name: Sarcoidosis - Lungs
DIA_SRCL.description: Sarcoidosis is the growth of granulomas (clustered inflammatory cells) in the lungs. Most likely it is due to an abnormal immune reaction to some agents. There is no cure, but with supporting medicine the disease progression can be halted.

DIA_SRCE.name: Sarcoidosis - Eye
DIA_SRCE.description: Sarcoidosis is the growth of granulomas (clustered inflammatory cells) in the eyes. Most likely it is due to an abnormal immune reaction to some agents. There is no cure, but with supporting medicine the disease progression can be halted.

DIA_SRCH.name: Sarcoidosis - Heart
DIA_SRCH.description: Sarcoidosis is the growth of granulomas (clustered inflammatory cells) in the heart. Most likely it is due to an abnormal immune reaction to some agents. There is no cure, but with supporting medicine the disease progression can be halted.

DIA_DM1.name: Diabetes
DIA_DM1.description: Diabetes mellitus refers to a group of diseases that affect the metabolism of glucose. There are many types of diabetes, but generally there are too high levels of glucose in the blood.

DIA_CFB.name: Cystic Fibrosis
DIA_CFB.description: Cystic fibrosis is an inherited disease that results in serious damage to the lungs due to recurrent pneumonias. Cystic fibrosis also results in a decreased uptake of nutrients from the gastrointestinal tract.

DIA_OTE.name: Otitis Externa
DIA_OTE.description: Otitis externa is the inflammation of the outer part of the ear canal, it is caused mostly by bacteria.

DIA_OME.name: Otitis Media
DIA_OME.description: Otitis media with effusion is a condition where fluid is present in the middle ear due to infection or inflammation following an infection. It usually affects children.

DIA_ASO.name: ASOM
DIA_ASO.description: Acute suppurative otitis media is a very painful infection of the middle ear.

DIA_AMT.name: Acute Mastoiditis
DIA_AMT.description: Mastoiditis refers to an infection and inflammation of the mastoid cells located in the mastoid process in the temporal bone (beneath the ear).

DIA_OTS.name: Otosclerosis
DIA_OTS.description: Otosclerosis refers to abnormal hardening of the bone tissue near the middle ear causing inability to transfer sounds from the middle ear to the inner ear; resulting in loss of hearing.

DIA_OOR.name: Otorrhea
DIA_OOR.description: Otorrhea is basically an inflammation of the ear.

DIA_PNM.name: Pneumonia
DIA_PNM.description: Pneumonia is an infection of the lungs. Pneumonia can either be mild or life threatening.

DIA_BPN.name: Bronchopneumonia
DIA_BPN.description: Bronchopneumonia is a type of pneumonia mainly affecting the bronchi.

DIA_BES.name: Bronchiectasis
DIA_BES.description: Bronchiectasis is a condition where the large airways in the lungs are damaged and enlarged.

DIA_SCB.name: Simple Chronic Bronchitis
DIA_SCB.description: Chronic bronchitis refers to a productive long-lasting cough along with constant irritation of the bronchial lining.

DIA_EMP.name: Emphysema
DIA_EMP.description: Emphysema refers to a condition when the alveoli - the air sacs - are damaged or destroyed, causing shortness of breath, even in states of relaxation.

DIA_CPD.name: COPD
DIA_CPD.description: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease - COPD is a chronic inflammatory lung disease that results in obstructed airways in the lungs.

DIA_ASM.name: Asthma
DIA_ASM.description: Asthma refers to a condition where the airways narrow, swell and produce extra mucus. It is a chronic respiratory disease and symptoms may vary from person to person and can be due to allergy.

DIA_PMX.name: Pneumothorax
DIA_PMX.description: Collapsed lung, also called pneumothorax, occurs when air escapes the lung, filling the space between the lung and the chest wall, preventing expansion of the affected lung.

DIA_CEL.name: Cellulitis
DIA_CEL.description: Cellulitis is a common bacterial infection of the skin caused mostly by streptococci and staphylococci species.

DIA_ADS.name: Atopic Dermatitis
DIA_ADS.description: Atopic dermatitis or eczema refers to a condition that makes skin reddish and itchy. It is chronic skin disease.

DIA_LUP.name: Lupus
DIA_LUP.description: Lupus is a systemic autoimmune disease. It is very difficult to diagnose.

TRM_PCT.name: Lung Contusion
TRM_PCT.description: Blunt injury or physical trauma to the chest either by impact or physical attack resulting in pulmonary contusion.

TRM_CMP.name: CO Poisoning
TRM_CMP.description: Carbon monoxide poisoning occurs from inhaling too much carbon monoxide.

TRM_CGP.name: Chlorine Gas Poisoning
TRM_CGP.description: Chlorine gas poisoning is a condition resulting from the inhalation of chlorine gas.

TRM_MTP.name: Methanol Poisoning
TRM_MTP.description: Methanol ingestion leads to an uncommon poisoning that can cause severe metabolic disturbances, blindness, permanent neurological dysfunction and death.

TRM_LDP.name: Lead Poisoning
TRM_LDP.description: Lead poisoning is a type of metal poisoning caused by the accumulation of lead in the body.

DIA_052.name: Pleurisy
DIA_052.description: Pleurisy refers to a condition when the membranes surrounding lungs become inflamed.

DIA_053.name: Asbestosis
DIA_053.description: Asbestosis refers to a condition that is caused by microscopic asbestos mineral deposits in the lungs.

DIA_054.name: Miliaria
DIA_054.description: Miliaria, also called "sweat rash", is a skin disease, common in hot and humid conditions.

DIA_055.name: Ciguatera
DIA_055.description: Ciguatera fish poisoning, also known as ciguatera is a food-borne disease caused by ingestion of fish flesh contaminated by certain toxins.

DIA_056.name: Pleural Empyema
DIA_056.description: Pleural empyema refers to an accumulation of pus in the pleural cavity, caused by a bacterial infection.

DIA_057.name: Chylothorax
DIA_057.description: Chylothorax refers to a condition when lymphatic fluid is accumulated in the pleural space.

DIA_058.name: Parapneumonic Effusion
DIA_058.description: Parapneumonic effusion refers to a condition that arises as a result of pneumonia, bronchiectasis or a lung abscess.

DIA_059.name: Cholesteatoma
DIA_059.description: Cholesteatoma is an noncancerous skin growth, often caused by repeated middle ear infections.

DIA_060.name: Croup
DIA_060.description: Croup is also known as laryngotracheobronchitis and it is usually caused by a virus, rarely by a bacterial infection.

DIA_061.name: Nasal Polyp
DIA_061.description: Nasal polyp is a non-cancerous growth in the nasal cavity.

DIA_062.name: Meniere's Disease
DIA_062.description: Meniere's disease is an inner ear disorder that is characterized by a vertigo with unclear cause.

DIA_063.name: Essential Thrombocythemia
DIA_063.description: Essential thrombocythemia refers to an uncommon condition when patient's body produces much more platelets than necessary.

DIA_064.name: Poison Ivy Rash
DIA_064.description: Poison ivy rash is an allergic reaction to resin urushiol that is located in the leaves, stems and roots of poison ivy, oak and sumac.

TRM_001.name: Hemothorax
TRM_001.description: Hemothorax is an accumulation of blood within the pleural cavity and is commonly caused by a chest injury.

TRM_002.name: Tension Pneumothorax
TRM_002.description: Tension pneumothorax occurs mostly in clinical situations such as ventilation, resuscitation or in people with lung disease.

DIA_ARR.name: Acute Rheumatic Fever
DIA_ARR.description: Acute rheumatic fever is an inflammatory disease affecting the heart, muscles, joints and the brain. Usually it develops after a simple streptococcal infection of the throat.

DIA_MST.name: Mitral Stenosis
DIA_MST.description: Mitral valve stenosis is a narrowing of the heart's mitral valve, that causes improper opening of the valve and thereby the blood flow through the valve is reduced.

DIA_TRS.name: Tricuspid Regurgitation
DIA_TRS.description: Improper closening of the heart's tricuspid valve, resulting in the regurgitation of blood (blood is pumped backwards, the wrong direction, because the valve can't close properly).

DIA_HHD.name: HHD With Heart Failure
DIA_HHD.description: Hypertensive heart disease refers to multiple complications related to high blood pressure that affects the heart and can result in heart failure.

DIA_IHD.name: IHD With Heart Failure
DIA_IHD.description: Ischaemic heart disease, also known as coronary artery disease, is large group of cardiovascular diseases caused by the atherosclerosis of the coronary arteries that can result in heart failure.

DIA_HRD.name: Hypertensive Renal Disease
DIA_HRD.description: Hypertensive renal disease refers to an increase in systemic blood pressure due to kidney injury or disease.

DIA_ANP.name: Angina Pectoris
DIA_ANP.description: Angina pectoris refers to chest pain caused by insufficient blood flow to the heart muscle. Angina pectoris is often one of the symptoms of coronary artery disease.

DIA_AMI.name: Myocardial Infarction
DIA_AMI.description: Acute myocardial infarction, also known as a heart attack, occurs when blood flow to the myocardium (the muscle tissue of the heart) is blocked. The affected muscle tissue can die and the patient is also at high risk of dying.

DIA_TAA.name: Thoracic Aortic Aneurysm
DIA_TAA.description: Thoracic aortic aneurysm refers is a condition where the part of the aorta in the chest cavity is dilated (becomes like a balloon). The aneurysm can rupture and lead to immediate death.

DIA_PEM.name: Pulmonary Embolism
DIA_PEM.description: Pulmonary embolism refers to a blockage in one of the pulmonary arteries. The embolism often comes from a dislodged deep venous thrombosis.

DIA_ECT.name: Endocarditis
DIA_ECT.description: Endocarditis is an infection of the inner lining of the heart. Often the infection arises from bacterial colonization of diseased valves of the heart

DIA_PCT.name: Pericarditis
DIA_PCT.description: Pericarditis refers to condition where the thin membrane, or sac, surrounding the heart is inflamed.

DIA_INM.name: Myocarditis
DIA_INM.description: Myocarditis is an inflammation of the heart's muscle, also known as myocardium, affecting the ability of the heart to pump blood properly and also the heart's electrical system, potentially giving rise to different arrhythmiaes (irregular and abnormal heart rythms).

DIA_CHF.name: CHF
DIA_CHF.description: Congestive heart failure where the heart loses its ability to pump a sufficient amount of blood into the body.

DIA_STK.name: Stroke
DIA_STK.description: Stroke occurs when the blood supply to an area of the brain is interrupted or severely reduced, resulting in the death (necrosis) of brain tissue in the affected area.

DIA_ATH.name: Atherosclerosis - Carotides
DIA_ATH.description: Atherosclerosis is a disease where plaques are built up inside the carotid arteries of the body (the large arteries running from the aorta to the head), hardening and narrowing them, thereby reducing the flow of blood. It could be asymptomatic for decades, symptoms often appear when arteries become more than 50% blocked.

DIA_TPB.name: Thrombophlebitis
DIA_TPB.description: Thrombophlebitis is condition where the inflammation of veins result in the formation of a blood clot that blocks one or more veins.

DIA_PVT.name: Portal Vein Thrombosis
DIA_PVT.description: Portal vein thrombosis refers to a condition where the portal vein (the large vein from the intestines to the liver) is blocked by blood clot.

DIA_PEB.name: Pulmonary Hypertension
DIA_PEB.description: Pulmonary hypertension is hypertension in the arteries from the heart to the lungs. Pulmonary hypertension often happens due to large or many small pulmonary emboli (blood clots in the lungs).

DIA_PAD.name: PAD
DIA_PAD.description: Peripheral artery disease is caused by a reduced blood flow to the upper or lower limbs due to narrowed arteries, often from artherosclerotic disease.

DIA_MVP.name: Mitral Valve Prolapse
DIA_MVP.description: Mitral valve prolapse is a condition where the heart's mitral valve bulges into the left atrium during the contraction of the left ventricle. This is usually following an acute myocardial infarction affecting the attachment of the valve in the left ventricle.

DIA_DCP.name: Dilated Cardiomyopathy
DIA_DCP.description: Dilated cardiomyopathy is a condition where the heart becomes severely enlarged, reducing the ability of the heart to pump, making the heart unable to effectively pump a sufficient amount of blood into the bloodstream.

DIA_GCA.name: Giant Cells Arteritis
DIA_GCA.description: Giant cells arteritis is an inflammation of the arterial lining, mainly in the temporal arteries on the side of the head.

TRM_BCI.name: Cardiac Blunt Injury
TRM_BCI.description: Cardiac blunt injury is physical trauma to a chest area, resulting in a trauma to the heart.

TRM_FLC.name: Flail Chest
TRM_FLC.description: Flail chest is a life-threatening condition that occurs when a segment of the rib cage breaks due to trauma and moves independently of the rest of the chest wall/ribs during breathing.

TRM_TCA.name: Traumatic Cardiac Arrest
TRM_TCA.description: Traumatic cardiac arrest is a condition where the heart has ceased to beat due to blunt or penetrating trauma, such as a stab wound hitting the heart. It is a surgical emergency which will always result in death without immediate surgical care.

TRM_TAR.name: Aortic Rupture
TRM_TAR.description: Traumatic aortic rupture is a condition where the aorta, the largest artery in the body, ruptures as a result of aortic disease (aortic aneurysm, aortic dissection) or trauma to the body. The condition is frequently fatal due to the massive bleeding that results from the rupture.

DIA_ECS.name: Encephalitis
DIA_ECS.description: Encephalitis is an inflammation of the brain, most commonly caused by an autoimmune reaction or viruses.

DIA_PMN.name: Pneumococcal Meningitis
DIA_PMN.description: Pneumococcal meningitis is an inflammation of membranes covering the spinal cord and brain due to the pneumococcal infection.

DIA_SMN.name: Staphylococcal Meningitis
DIA_SMN.description: Staphylococcal meningitis is an inflammation of membranes covering the spinal cord and brain due to the staphylococcal infection.

DIA_MIG.name: Migraine
DIA_MIG.description: Migraine is a severe, painful headache with or without aura.

DIA_GBS.name: Guillain-Barre Syndrome
DIA_GBS.description: Guillain-Barre syndrome is a serious disease where the immune system attacks its own nervous system.

DIA_NMO.name: Neuromyelitis Optica
DIA_NMO.description: Neuromyelitis optica is an auto-immune disease where the immune system attacks the eye nerves and the spinal cord. A condition that is often seen because of multiple sclerosis.

DIA_IPD.name: CIPD
DIA_IPD.description: Chronic inflammatory demyelinizating polyneuropathy is an immune inflammatory disease affecting peripheral nervous system by destructing the cover sheet that protect and insulate the nerves and fibres.

DIA_TTN.name: Tetanus
DIA_TTN.description: Tetanus is a serious bacterial disease affecting the nervous system.

DIA_CLH.name: Cluster Headache
DIA_CLH.description: Cluster headaches are extremely painful headaches, often with crying and a dripping nose, that only occur on one side of the head.

DIA_HRP.name: Hypertensive Retinopathy
DIA_HRP.description: Hypertensive retinopathy is damage to the retina and retina’s blood vessels due to high blood pressure.

DIA_BLP.name: Blepharitis
DIA_BLP.description: Blepharitis is inflammation of the eyelid.

DIA_CJN.name: Conjunctivitis (bacterial)
DIA_CJN.description: Conjunctivitis  known as "Pink eye" is an inflammatory disease of the outermost layer of the white part of the eye.

DIA_CAN.name: Conjunctivitis (allergic)
DIA_CAN.description: Conjunctivitis  known as "Pink eye" is an inflammatory disease of the outermost layer of the white part of the eye.

DIA_KRT.name: Keratitis - Bacterial
DIA_KRT.description: Keratitis is an inflammatory disease, mostly caused by bacteria, of the cornea - the clear dome surface of the eye.

DIA_CAB.name: Corneal Abrasion
DIA_CAB.description: Corneal abrasion - known also as scratched eye is one of the most common eye injuries, when foreign obstacles scratches the eye and produce discomfort and related symptoms.

DIA_UVE.name: Uveitis
DIA_UVE.description: Uveitis refers to inflammation of the part of the eye called the uvea. The uvea includes the iris and some adjacent structures.

DIA_SCL.name: Scleritis
DIA_SCL.description: Scleritis refers to inflammation of the sclera of the eye.

DIA_GLC.name: Open-Angle Glaucoma
DIA_GLC.description: Glaucoma is a disease that results in damage to the optic nerve, because of high pressure inside the eye .

DIA_ACG.name: Angle-Closure Glaucoma
DIA_ACG.description: Acute angle-closure glaucoma is caused by sudden and rapid increase of the intraocular pressure.

DIA_CRV.name: CRVO
DIA_CRV.description: Central retinal vein occlusion is one of the common vascular disorders of the eye's retina.

TRM_CCS.name: Concussion
TRM_CCS.description: A concussion is a mild traumatic brain injury resulting in temporary altered mental state and/or loss of function.

TRM_CTS.name: Cerebral Contusion
TRM_CTS.description: Cerebral contusion is a traumatic brain injury usually due to blunt head trauma.

TRM_DAI.name: Diffuse Axonal Injury
TRM_DAI.description: Diffuse axonal injury is one of the most common head traumas, it occurs when the brain shifts too quickly inside the skull.

TRM_TBI.name: Traumatic Brain Injury
TRM_TBI.description: Occurs when an external force injures the brain. It can be caused by accidents or blunt trauma.

TRM_AIH.name: Subdural Hematoma
TRM_AIH.description: Subdural hematoma is a condition where veins in the subdural space are torn and start to bleed. Blood accumulates in the narrow space which increases the pressure inside the skull cavity. The condition is often fatal if left untreated.

TRM_OBI.name: Penetrating Trauma - Head
TRM_OBI.description: Penetrating head injury is an injury in which the dura mater, the outer layer of the meninges, is breached.

DIA_051.name: TIA
DIA_051.description: TIA - Transient ischemic attack is similar to a stroke, but usually lasts  up to 24h and causes no permanent damage.

DIA_049.name: PCF
DIA_049.description: PCF - Pharyngoconjunctival fever refers to a condition mostly caused by Human mastadenovirus B, that causes small outbreaks mostly between young people in collectives. The disease affects pharynx and eyes.

DIA_050.name: Botulism
DIA_050.description: Botulism is a rare and potentially fatal illness caused by toxins produced by bacterium Clostridium botulinum.

DIA_CTS.name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
DIA_CTS.description: A condition where the median nerve in the wrist is compressed by the surrounding structures when passing through the tunnel near the base of the wrist. Pain and a sleeping sensation of the hand are the main symptoms.

DIA_CUS.name: Cubital Tunnel Syndrome
DIA_CUS.description: Cubital tunnel syndrome is caused by the compression of the ulnar nerve in the elbow area.

DIA_RAT.name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
DIA_RAT.description: Rheumatoid arthritis is a chronic autoimmune disease affecting various joints. It typically results in warm, swollen, and painful joints. Pain and stiffness often worsen following rest.

DIA_HOA.name: Hip Osteoarthritis
DIA_HOA.description: Osteoarthritis is a condition where cartilage slowly degenerates and deformities arise in the affected joint. Hip osteoarthritis typically makes walking painful and cumbersome.

DIA_KOA.name: Knee Osteoarthritis
DIA_KOA.description: Knee Osteoarthritis is one of the most common types of arthritis. A slow degradation of the knee cartilage causes pain when walking and stiffness of the joint.

DIA_DMS.name: Dermatomyositis
DIA_DMS.description: Dermatomyositis is chronic progressive inflammation of the muscles. Symptoms are usually a skin rash and increased muscle weakness over time.

DIA_IBM.name: Inclusion-Body Myositis
DIA_IBM.description: Inclusion-body myositis is chronic progressive inflammation and weakening of the muscles. It is most apparent the finger flexor and knee extensor muscles.

DIA_PMS.name: Polymyositis
DIA_PMS.description: Polymyositis is chronic progressive inflammation and weakening of the muscles. It begins with weakness in the muscles closest to the body trunk from where it spreads.

DIA_SDS.name: Spondylolysis
DIA_SDS.description: Spondylolysis is a defect, crack or stress fracture in one of the vertebrae, typically in the L5-S1 level. The condition can be painful.

DIA_SDL.name: Spondylolisthesis
DIA_SDL.description: Spondylolisthesis refers to a displaced vertebrae. Several conditions can cause spondylolisthesis; degenerative changes, trauma, congenital abnormalities, malignancy etc.

DIA_CSL.name: Cervical Spondylosis
DIA_CSL.description: Cervical spondylosis is a degeneration of the cervical spine from any cause. Often it is synonymous with age-related wear and tear causing spinal osteoarthritis.

DIA_OSM.name: Acute Osteomyelitis
DIA_OSM.description: Acute osteomyelitis is a bacterial infection of the bone. It can develop into chronic osteomyelitis which is very difficult to treat.

DIA_CDD.name: Spinal Stenosis
DIA_CDD.description: Spinal stenosis refers to an abnormal narrowing of the spinal canal, which affects the spinal cord or nerve roots. It causes pain, discomfort and loss of mobility usually in the limbs corresponding to the level of the stenosis.

DIA_BOH.name: Hand Bursitis
DIA_BOH.description: A bursae is a small fluid filled sac which acts as a cushion between bone and areas prone to friction. Bursitis refers to swelling of said sac caused by inflammation

DIA_AOM.name: Osteomalacia
DIA_AOM.description: Osteomalacia, known as Rickets if it occurs in childhood, refers to a condition where the bones soften due to impaired bone metabolism.

DIA_OLB.name: Elbow Bursitis
DIA_OLB.description: A bursae is a small fluid filled sac which acts as a cushion between bone and areas prone to friction. Bursitis refers to swelling of said sac caused by inflammation.

DIA_BOT.name: Knee Bursitis
DIA_BOT.description: A bursae is a small fluid filled sac which acts as a cushion between bone and areas prone to friction. Bursitis refers to swelling of said sac caused by inflammation.

DIA_TEW.name: Tennis Elbow
DIA_TEW.description: Tennis elbow, also known as lateral epicondylitis, is characterized by radiating pain from the outside of the elbow to the wrist. The condition is caused by performing repetitive movement and, thus, irritation of the muscles and tendons associated with said movement.

TRM_DWH.name: Deep Wound on Hand
TRM_DWH.description: A bleeding deep, smooth or jagged wound. It needs suturing.

TRM_DWA.name: Deep Wound on Arm
TRM_DWA.description: A bleeding deep, smooth or jagged wound. It needs suturing.

TRM_DWL.name: Deep Wound on Leg
TRM_DWL.description: A bleeding deep, smooth or jagged wound. It needs suturing..

TRM_DWF.name: Deep Wound on Feet
TRM_DWF.description: A bleeding deep, smooth or jagged wound. It needs suturing.

TRM_SHS.name: Shoulder Sprain
TRM_SHS.description: A sprain is a condition where a ligament surrounding a joint is taken beyond its functional range of motion, causing tears and sometimes to complete rupture of the affected ligaments.

TRM_SHD.name: Shoulder Dislocation
TRM_SHD.description: Joint dislocation, also known as luxation, is a condition where the bones connected by a joint are abnormally separated. Damage to the adjacent tissue is common.

TRM_KSP.name: Knee Sprain
TRM_KSP.description: A sprain is a condition where a ligament surrounding a joint is taken beyond its functional range of motion, causing tears and sometimes to complete rupture of the affected ligaments.

TRM_KDL.name: Knee Dislocation
TRM_KDL.description: Joint dislocation, also known as luxation, is a condition where the bones connected by a joint are abnormally separated. Damage to the adjacent tissue is common.

TRM_ASP.name: Ankle Sprain
TRM_ASP.description: A sprain is a condition where a ligament surrounding a joint is taken beyond its functional range of motion, causing tears and sometimes to complete rupture of the affected ligaments.

TRM_DLA.name: Dislocated Ankle
TRM_DLA.description: Joint dislocation, also known as luxation, is a condition where the bones connected by a joint are abnormally separated. Damage to the adjacent tissue is common.

TRM_ACL.name: ACL Injury
TRM_ACL.description: The Anterior Cruciate Ligament, is a ligament located inside the knee joint that prevents forward displacement between the leg bones. Injury to this ligament is common in sports and is caused by abnormal inward rotation.

TRM_SFU.name: Simple Fracture of Ulna
TRM_SFU.description: Fracture of ulna, one of the two bones in the forearm, without damage to the surrounding tissues or breaking of the skin.

TRM_OFU.name: Open Fracture of Ulna
TRM_OFU.description: Open fracture, also called compound fracture, is a fracture resulting in an open wound, cause by the dislocated bone, often with bone protruding through the wound. Ulna is one of the two bones in the forearm.

TRM_CFU.name: Complicated Fracture of Ulna
TRM_CFU.description: Complicated fracture consists of open wound, dislocated fractured bone and various fragments of broken bone. Ulna is one of the two bones in the forearm.

TRM_SFR.name: Simple Fracture of Radius
TRM_SFR.description: Fracture of radius, one of the two bones in the forearm, without damage to the surrounding tissues or breaking of the skin.

TRM_SFH.name: Simple Fracture of Humerus
TRM_SFH.description: Fracture of humerus, the bone in the upper arm, without damage to the surrounding tissues or breaking of the skin.

TRM_OFH.name: Open Fracture of Humerus
TRM_OFH.description: Open fracture, also called compound fracture, is a fracture resulting in an open wound, cause by the dislocated bone, often with bone protruding through the wound. Humerus is the bone in the upper arm.

TRM_CFH.name: Complicated Fracture of Humerus
TRM_CFH.description: Complicated fracture consists of open wound, dislocated fractured bone and various fragments of broken bone. Humerus is the bone in the upper arm.

TRM_BRR.name: Broken Ribs
TRM_BRR.description: Simple fracture of one or more ribs with no damage to surrounding tissues or breaking of the skin.

TRM_SFF.name: Simple Fracture of Femur
TRM_SFF.description: Fracture of the femur, the bone in the thigh, without damage to the surrounding tissues or breaking of the skin.

TRM_OFF.name: Open Fracture of Femur
TRM_OFF.description: Open fracture, also called compound fracture, is a fracture resulting in an open wound, cause by the dislocated bone, often with bone protruding through the wound. The femur is the bone in the thigh.

TRM_CFF.name: Complicated Fracture of Femur
TRM_CFF.description: Complicated fracture consists of open wound, dislocated fractured bone and various fragments of broken bone. The femur is the bone in the thigh.

TRM_SFT.name: Simple Fracture of Tibia
TRM_SFT.description: Fracture of tibia, the large bone in the lower leg, without damage to the surrounding tissues or breaking of the skin.

TRM_SPT.name: Spiral Fracture of Tibia
TRM_SPT.description: Special spiral fracture of tibia bone, with no damage to surrounding tissues.

TRM_SFI.name: Simple Fracture of Fibula
TRM_SFI.description: Fracture of fibula, the thin bone in the lower leg, without damage to the surrounding tissues or breaking of the skin.

TRM_OFT.name: Open Fracture of Tibia
TRM_OFT.description: Open fracture, also called compound fracture, is a fracture resulting in an open wound, cause by the dislocated bone, often with bone protruding through the wound. Tibia is the large bone in the lower leg.

TRM_ANF.name: Ankle Fracture
TRM_ANF.description: Fracture of one or more bones of the ankle, without damage to the surrounding tissues.

TRM_MBF.name: Metatarsal Bones Fracture
TRM_MBF.description: Fracture of one or more metatarsal bones, without damage to the surrounding tissues.

TRM_FOF.name: Fracture of Finger
TRM_FOF.description: Fracture of one or more of the finger bones, without damage to the surrounding tissues.

TRM_SFC.name: Simple Fracture of Clavicle
TRM_SFC.description: Fracture of the clavicle without damage to the surrounding tissues or breaking of the skin.

TRM_SHC.name: Shaft Fracture of Clavicle
TRM_SHC.description: Fracture of the clavicle with dislocation of part of the fractured bone.

TRM_MNF.name: Mandibular Fracture
TRM_MNF.description: Fracture of the jaw. Often includes a break in two places. Usually causes decreased ability to open the mouth fully.

TRM_NSF.name: Nasal Fracture
TRM_NSF.description: Simple fracture of one or more nasal bones with some bleeding present.

DIA_EPI.name: Exocrine Pancreatic Insufficiency
DIA_EPI.description: Pancreatic insufficiency occurs when the gland is damaged enough to stop functioning sufficiently. Normal absorption of nutrients in the small intestine is thus compromised resulting in nutritional deficiencies.

DIA_THD.name: Hashimoto's Thyroiditis
DIA_THD.description: Hashimoto's thyroiditis is an autoimmune disease where the immune system gradually destroys the thyroid gland.

DIA_HPT.name: Hypothyroidism
DIA_HPT.description: Hypothyroidism is a disease where the thyroid gland functions inadequately and produces too little thyroid hormone.

DIA_HRT.name: Grave's Disease
DIA_HRT.description: Grave's disease is an endocrine disease where the thyroid gland produces thyroid hormones in excess.

DIA_OES.name: Esophagitis
DIA_OES.description: Esophagitis is an inflammation of the oesophageal lining, often caused by acid reflux.

DIA_OEV.name: Esophageal Varices
DIA_OEV.description: Esophageal varices are abnormally enlarged veins within the esophagus. They are usually caused by portal hypertension due to existing liver disease.

DIA_HBP.name: H. Pylori Infection
DIA_HBP.description: Helicobacter pylori is a bacteria present in the upper gastrointestinal tract. It is the main cause of peptic ulcers.

DIA_GAU.name: Peptic Ulcer Disease
DIA_GAU.description: A peptic ulcer is an open lesion in the lining of the stomach, duodenum or esophagus. It is most commonly caused by the bacterium h. pylori or prolonged, excessive use of NSAIDs

DIA_GAS.name: Gastritis
DIA_GAS.description: Gastritis is an inflammation or erosion of the stomach's lining. It is caused by; bad nutrition, excessive alcohol use, or excessive use of some medications. Treatment consists of protone pump inhibitors and discontinuation of alcohol or drugs that cause the symptoms.

DIA_DUO.name: Duodenitis
DIA_DUO.description: Doudenitis is an inflammation of the lining of the duodenum; the first part of the small intestine, which is 12 inches long and curved into the C-shape.

DIA_APP.name: Appendicitis
DIA_APP.description: Appendicitis refers to infection in the appendix, which becomes obstructed and swollen. A ruptured appendix can be fatal and thus appendicitis is one of the most common reasons for abdominal surgery.

DIA_CRH.name: Crohn's Disease
DIA_CRH.description: Crohn's disease is an inflammatory bowel disease that affects the entire intestine. The exact cause is unknown, however, it is evident that there are genetic, environmental and autoimmune components.

DIA_ULC.name: Ulcerative Colitis
DIA_ULC.description: Ulcerative colitis is an inflammatory bowel disease that produces ulcers in the large intestine. The exact cause is unknown, however, it is evident that there are genetic, environmental and autoimmune components.

DIA_IBS.name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome
DIA_IBS.description: Irritable bowel syndrome is chronic gastrointestinal disorder demanding a long term treatment approach. It is not contagious. Diet modification and medicine can usually relieve symptoms.

DIA_FDR.name: Bacterial Gastroenteritis
DIA_FDR.description: Bacterial gastroenteritis is common gastrointestinal disease, in most cases lasting a few days.

DIA_FOI.name: Fistula of the Intestine
DIA_FOI.description: A fistula is an abnormal anatomical connection between two hollow spaces.

DIA_PER.name: Primary Peritonitis
DIA_PER.description: Primary peritonitis is a bacterial infection of the peritoneum; a membrane covering the inner abdominal wall and organs within the abdominal cavity. Left untreated the condition is almost always fatal.

DIA_HEF.name: Hepatic Fibrosis
DIA_HEF.description: Hepatic fibrosis represents an accumulation of scar tissue in the liver. The scar tissue can be the result of long term inflammation or another liver disease caused by viruses, bacterias, malnutrition or alcohol abuse etc. If left untreated it causes cirrhosis.

DIA_CIR.name: Cirrhosis
DIA_CIR.description: Cirrhosis describes a condition where the liver is malfunctioning due to long-term damage; normal liver tissue is replaced with scar tissue.

DIA_CLT.name: Gallstones
DIA_CLT.description: Gallstones, also called cholelithiasis, are concretions of  usually made from bilirubin, bile acids and calcium formed in the gall bladder. Gallstones usually do not cause symptoms, however, if they block the bile duct they can cause a variety of serious conditions.

DIA_CLS.name: Cholecystitis
DIA_CLS.description: Cholecystitis is inflammation and infection of the gallbladder, usually due to blockage of the cystic duct by a gallstone.

DIA_CLA.name: Cholangitis
DIA_CLA.description: Cholangitis is an infection of the bile duct usually caused by bacteria ascending from the duodenum. It occurs usually when the bile duct is already partially blocked by gallstones.

DIA_APT.name: Pancreatitis
DIA_APT.description: Pancreatitis refers to inflammation of the pancreas. It is usually caused by gallstone blockage or excessive alcohol abuse.

DIA_COE.name: Coeliac Disease
DIA_COE.description: Coeliac disease is an autoimmune disorder where the immune system reacts to dietary gluten present in wheat, rye and barley. The reaction causes inflammation in the small intestines and thus malabsorption of nutrients.

DIA_ANS.name: Glomerulonephritis
DIA_ANS.description: Glomerulonephritis refers to inflammation of the filtering unit of the kidneys. It can be caused by an infection or autoimmunity.

DIA_TIN.name: Interstitial Nephritis
DIA_TIN.description: Interstitial nephritis is an inflammation of the interstitial tissue of the kidneys. It can be caused by an infection or as an adverse reaction to medication.

DIA_PNS.name: Pyelonephritis
DIA_PNS.description: Pyelonephritis is inflammation of the kidney, usually due to ascending bacteria spreading up through the urinary tract.

DIA_ARF.name: Acute Renal Failure
DIA_ARF.description: Acute renal failure refers to an abrupt loss of kidney function, effectively seizing the ability to filter the blood and produce urine. The causes can be many, but are usually due to extensive tissue damage or decreased kidney blood flow.

DIA_CKD.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
DIA_CKD.description: Chronic kidney disease refers to a gradual loss of kidney function. The most common cause is untreated diabetes mellitus.

DIA_COK.name: Kidney Stones
DIA_COK.description: Kidney stones, also known as urolithiasis, are usually concretions of calcium. Even though the stones usually pass on their own through the urinary tract, it is an extremely painful condition.

DIA_COU.name: Calculus of Ureter
DIA_COU.description: Kidney stones, also known as urolithiasis, are usually concretions of calcium. Even though the stones usually pass on their own through the urinary tract, they can sometimes get stuck in the ureter, which is an extremely painful condition.

DIA_CIB.name: Calculus in Bladder
DIA_CIB.description: Bladder stones can be formed in the bladder due to a mineralization of concentrated urine. There will only be symptoms if the flow of urine is compromised.

DIA_ACS.name: Urinary Tract Infection
DIA_ACS.description: UTF, or cystitis, is an infection of the bladder usually by E. coli; a type of bacteria commonly found in the gastrointestinal (GI) tract. It is a condition a hundred times more common in women due to a shorter urinary tract.

DIA_INC.name: Interstitial Cystitis
DIA_INC.description: Interstitial cystitis is also called painful bladder syndrome.

TRM_LIP.name: Liver Laceration
TRM_LIP.description: Liver laceration occurs when an object pierces the tissue of the body and tears the liver.

TRM_LIB.name: Liver Contusion
TRM_LIB.description: Liver contusion happens due to physical trauma to the upper abdomen.

TRM_SRB.name: Blunt Spleen Rupture
TRM_SRB.description: Blunt spleen rupture is caused by impact, injury or physical attack, but with no presence of an open wound.

TRM_SRP.name: Penetrated Spleen Rupture
TRM_SRP.description: Penetrated spleen rupture happens when an object pierces the skin of upper abdomen, causing an open wound and spleen damage.

TRM_SCB.name: Spleen Contusion
TRM_SCB.description: Spleen contusion refers to bruising of the spleen after a direct impact to the upper abdomen.

TRM_PCB.name: Pancreas Contusion
TRM_PCB.description: Pancreas contusion happens due to physical trauma to the upper abdomen.

TRM_PLB.name: Blunt Pancreas Laceration
TRM_PLB.description: Blunt pancreas laceration is caused by impact, injury or physical attack, but with no presence of an open wound.

TRM_PCP.name: Penetrated Pancreas Laceration
TRM_PCP.description: Penetrated pancreas laceration happens when an object pierces the skin of upper abdomen, causing an open wound and pancreas damage.

TRM_KLP.name: Renal Laceration
TRM_KLP.description: Renal laceration occurs when an object pierces the tissue of the body and tears the kidney.

TRM_KCB.name: Renal Contusion
TRM_KCB.description: Renal contusion refers to bruising of the kidney (latin: ren) after a direct impact to the upper lower back.

DIA_047.name: HUS
DIA_047.description: Hemolytic uremic syndrome refers to the condition when small blood vessels in kidneys become damaged and inflamed. In most cases an infection of Escherichia coli is responsible.

DIA_048.name: Campylobacteriosis
DIA_048.description: Campylobacteriosis refers to a common infection caused most commonly by Campylobacter jejuni. It is often a food-borne illness.

DIA_INFX.name: Influenza
DIA_INFX.description: Influenza, commonly known as 'the flu', is a common infectious disease caused by the influenza virus family.

DIA_HEPAX.name: Hepatitis A
DIA_HEPAX.description: Hepatitis refers to a viral infection of the liver. Hepatitis A virus spreads through feces and usually occurs in areas with poor sanitation or hygiene.

DIA_HEPBX.name: Hepatitis B
DIA_HEPBX.description: Hepatitis refers to a viral infection of the liver. Hepatitis B virus spreads via blood, sexual contact or from mother to child.

DIA_CLCX.name: Cholera
DIA_CLCX.description: Cholera is a bacterial disease that usually spread through contaminated water. Modern sewage and water treatment eliminated cholera in industrialized countries.

DIA_TYFX.name: Typhoid Fever
DIA_TYFX.description: Typhoid fever is a bacterial disease that spreads through contaminated water and food. It is rare in industrialized countries.

DIA_TBCX.name: Tuberculosis
DIA_TBCX.description: Tuberculosis of the lungs is a serious disease caused by the bacteria Mycobacterium tuberculosis affecting mainly lungs, but it can spread to other organs such as the brain.

DIA_TULX.name: Tularaemia
DIA_TULX.description: Tularaemia is an infectious disease that mainly affects the skin, eyes, lymph nodes or lungs. It is caused by the Francisella tularensis bacteria. Tulareamia is highly contagious, potentially fatal, but can be treated effectively.

DIA_PNAX.name: Pulmonary Anthrax
DIA_PNAX.description: Anthrax is serious infection caused by the bacterium Bacillus anthracis. It is spread through the bacteria's spores. Contact is made typically by inhaling or eating the spores or through skin lesions.

DIA_RBFX.name: Rat-Bite Fever
DIA_RBFX.description: Rat-bite fever is an acute febrile disease caused by bacteria transmitted by rodents, particularly by their urine or mucous secretions.

DIA_CSDX.name: Cat-Scratch Disease
DIA_CSDX.description: Cat-scratch disease is a common infectious disease caused by Bartonella henselae. It is transmitted by cats, through the cat's scratch or bite. Presence of the bacteria in humans is hard to detect by traditional bacterial cultivation because growth takes up to 6 weeks.

DIA_PTSX.name: Pertussis
DIA_PTSX.description: Pertussis also known as Whooping Cough is a contagious respiratory disease. It is caused by the bacteria Bordetella pertussis. Before the development of a vaccine, pertussis was considered a childhood disease. Now it affects people whose immunedefense is weakened.

DIA_LEGX.name: Legionnaires Disease
DIA_LEGX.description: Legionnaires disease is caused by the bacterium Legionella pneumophila. The disease develops into a severe form of pneumonia, transmitting through person to person. Untreated Legionnaires diseases are usually fatal.

DIA_TPFX.name: Typhus Fever
DIA_TPFX.description: Important not to be confused with Typhoid fever. Typhus is caused by the bacteria Rickettsia Typhi, transmitted through various types of fleas. Typhus was first described in 1528. It has a sudden onset of various symptoms comparable to the common flu.

DIA_ATSX.name: American Trypanosomias
DIA_ATSX.description: American trypanosomias also known as Chagas disease is serious infectious disease caused by the parasite Trypanosoma cruzi. If left untreated, it can lead to serious heart and digestive complications.

DIA_TXMX.name: Toxoplasmosis
DIA_TXMX.description: Toxoplasmosis is caused by the parasite Toxoplasma gondii. Symptoms are similar to the common flu. The parasite is spread through undercooked food and exposure to cat's feces.

DIA_BABX.name: Babesiosis
DIA_BABX.description: Babesiosis is parasitic disease caused by the parasite Babesia. It is comparable to malaria. It spreads through ticks and often presents with Lyme disease.

DIA_LYDX.name: Lyme Disease
DIA_LYDX.description: Lyme disease is caused by the bite of a deer tick or back-legged tick. It can transfer a bacteria, Borrelia burgdorferi, which directly causes disease.

DIA_MUMX.name: Mumps
DIA_MUMX.description: Mumps also known as Parotitis, is an inflammatory, infectious disease affecting the parotid glands (on one or on both sides of the head). The inflammation is caused by bacterial or viral agents. It is best prevented by vaccination.

DIA_TBEX.name: Tick-Borne Encephalitis
DIA_TBEX.description: Tick-borne encephalitis is a viral tick-borne infectious disease involving brain and central nervous system.

DIA_RABX.name: Rabies
DIA_RABX.description: Rabies is a deadly virus spread by saliva of infected animals. Transmission is often through animal bites.

DIA_001.name: Anaplasmosis
DIA_001.description: Anaplasmosis is caused by bacteria Anaplasmosis phagocytophilum that is spread by ticks.

DIA_002.name: Ehrlichiosis
DIA_002.description: Ehrlichiosis is a flu-like disease that is closely related to Anaplasmosis. It is also spread by ticks.

DIA_003.name: Boutonneuse Fever
DIA_003.description: Boutonneuse fever is a fever caused by rickettsial infection caused by Rickettsia conorii and it is transmitted by the dog tick. The disease occurrs mainly in the Mediterranean basin, Middle East, Indian subcontinent and Africa.

DIA_004.name: Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever
DIA_004.description: Bacterial disease caused by Rickettsia rickettsii, spread by various species of ticks (american dog tick, rocky mountain wood tick, brown dog tick).

DIA_005.name: Colorado Tick Fever
DIA_005.description: Viral infection spread by Rocky Mountain wood tick, symptoms are very similar to Rocky Mountain spotted fever.

DIA_006.name: Pacific Coast Tick Fever
DIA_006.description: Bacterial disease caused by Rickettsia philipii, spread by Pacific coast tick.

DIA_007.name: African Tick Bite Fever
DIA_007.description: African tick bite fever - ATBF is a bacterial infection caused by Rickettsia africae, spread by ticks. The disease occurs in Sub-Saharan Africa, Carribean and Oceania.

DIA_008.name: Queensland Tick Typhus
DIA_008.description: The etiologic agent of Queensland tick typhus is bacteria Rickettsia australis. The disease occurs only in Eastern Australia, including Tasmania.

DIA_009.name: Flinders Island Spotted Fever
DIA_009.description: Flinders Island spotted fever is caused by Rickettsia honei and the disease occurs mainly in southeast and south Asia and also in Australia.

DIA_010.name: Japanese Spotted Fever
DIA_010.description: Japanese spotted fever occurs mainly in warm areas along the Pacific side of southwest and central Japan. The disease is caused by Rickettsia japonica, transmitted via a tick bite.

DIA_011.name: TIBOLA
DIA_011.description: Tick-borne lymphadenopathy is a tick-borne rickettsiosis relatively common in central Europe and casued by Rickettsia slovaca.

DIA_012.name: Rickettsialpox
DIA_012.description: Rickettsialpox is a mite-borne infectious disease caused by Rickettsia akari, that is spread by mites usually living on house mice.

DIA_013.name: Brucellosis
DIA_013.description: Brucellosis is disease caused by Brucella species bacteria and it is spread by ingestion of unpasterized milk or undercooked meat.

DIA_014.name: Chikungunya
DIA_014.description: Chikungunya virus infection is spread by the bite of an infected mosquito. There is no vaccine.

DIA_015.name: Q - Fever
DIA_015.description: Q fever is a disease caused by Coxiella burnetti and it is spread by various animals like cattle, sheep, goats, cats and dogs. The bacterium is an intracellular parasite, capable of growth and reproduction inside the hosts cells.

DIA_016.name: Dengue Fever
DIA_016.description: Dengue fever is a tropical disease transmitted by infected mosquito's bite.

DIA_017.name: Zika Fever
DIA_017.description: Zika fever is tropical disease caused by Zika virus and it is spread by the Aedes mosquitos.

DIA_018.name: Ebola
DIA_018.description: Ebola virus disease is a deadly viral hemorrhagic fever. It is caused by ebolaviruses, spread by direct contact with infected body fluids.

DIA_019.name: Malaria
DIA_019.description: Malaria is a tropical infectious disease caused by parasites from the plasmodium group. It is spread via infected mosquito bites.

DIA_020.name: Yellow Fever
DIA_020.description: Yellow fever is a tropical viral disease that is spread by the bite of an infected female mosquito. It is caused by yellow fever virus, enveloped RNA virus of the family Flaviridae.

DIA_021.name: West Nile Fever
DIA_021.description: West Nile fever is an infection caused by the West Nile virus, the disease is spread by infected mosquitos. Most people do not develop any symptoms. The virus was discovered in Uganda in 1937.

DIA_022.name: Lassa Fever
DIA_022.description: Also known as Lassa hemorrhagic fever is caused by the Lassa virus that is spread specifically by African rats.

DIA_023.name: Marburg
DIA_023.description: Marburg virus disease is a severe disease that affects humans and non-human primates, caused by either of two marburgviruses - Marburg virus and Ravn virus.

DIA_024.name: Leptospirosis
DIA_024.description: Leptospirosis is a blood infection caused by bacteria Leptospira and it is typically spread by rodents.

DIA_025.name: C. Difficile Infection
DIA_025.description: Clostridium difficile infection is caused by toxin-producing bacteria. It can be fatal.

DIA_026.name: Valley Fever
DIA_026.description: Valley fever, also called Coccidioidomycosis, is an infection caused by fungus Coccidioides that lives in the soil of the southwestern US, Mexico and central and South America.

DIA_027.name: COVID-19
DIA_027.description: COVID-19 is the disease caused by the novel coronavirus SARS-CoV-2 and causes a respiratory infection. First identified in Wu-han, China in December 2019, the disease spread world-wide, causing the biggest pandemic since the 1918 Spanish flu pandemic.

DIA_028.name: MERS
DIA_028.description: MERS - Middle East respiratory syndrome, also known as Camel flu, is a highly contagious viral respiratory infection caused by MERS-coronavirus.

DIA_029.name: SARS
DIA_029.description: SARS - Severe acute respiratory syndrome is a viral respiratory disease caused by the SARS coronavirus. The first outbreak occurred in southern China between November 2002 and July 2003.

DIA_030.name: Respiratory Crypto
DIA_030.description: Cryptosporidiosis is a parasitic disease caused by protozoan parasites named Cryptosporidium. It affects the respiratory system and also the digestive tract.

DIA_031.name: Intestinal Crypto
DIA_031.description: Cryptosporidiosis is a parasitic disease caused by protozoan parasites named Cryptosporidium. It affects the digestive apparatus and also the respiratory system.

DIA_032.name: HPS
DIA_032.description: HPS - Hantavirus Pulmonary syndrome is a serious viral respiratory disease caused by hantaviruses spread by rodents.

DIA_033.name: Norovirus
DIA_033.description: Norovirus, also called winter vomiting bug is a virus that causes diarrhea and vomiting. People that are infected can shed billions of norovirus particles.

DIA_034.name: Rotavirus A
DIA_034.description: Rotavirus A causes watery diarrhea. The best prevention is vaccination.

DIA_035.name: Melioidosis
DIA_035.description: Melioidosis, also called Whitmore's disease is an infectious disease caused by Burkholderia pseudomallei and it is transmitted by both animals and contaminated soil or water.

DIA_036.name: Respiratory Syncytial Virus
DIA_036.description: HRSV - also known as Human orthopneumovirus is a syncytial virus causing respiratory infections.

DIA_037.name: Lymphocytic Choriomeningitis
DIA_037.description: Lymphocytic choriomeningitis is a rodent-borne viral infectious disease caused by lymphocytic choriomeningitis virus, spread commonly by house mice.

DIA_038.name: Bubonic Plague
DIA_038.description: Bubonic plague is one of the three types of plague caused by bacterium Yersinia pestis. Bubonic plague is spread by infected fleas living on small animals. It is specific in its infection of lymph nodes, that become extremely swollen.

DIA_039.name: Septicemic Plague
DIA_039.description: Septicemic plague is one of the three types of plague caused by bacterium Yersinia pestis. Septicemic plague is spread by infected fleas living on small animals. It is the rarest of all three forms, typically infecting the blood.

DIA_040.name: Pneumonic Plague
DIA_040.description: Pneumonic plague is one of the three types of plague caused by bacterium Yersinia pestis. It is spread by infected fleas living on small animals. The pneumonic form causes a pulmonary infection.

DIA_041.name: Nipah Virus Infection
DIA_041.description: Nipah virus infection is a serious and potentially fatal disease, caused by an RNA virus in the genus Henipavirus. It spreads between people and can also transmit from animals to humans.

DIA_042.name: Rift Valley Fever
DIA_042.description: Rift Valley fever is a viral disease, caused by RVF virus that is transmitted via animal body fluids.

DIA_043.name: Relapsing Fever
DIA_043.description: Relapsing fever is an infectious vectro-borne disease, caused by a group of bacteria in the genus Borelia and it is transmitted via bites of lice or soft-bodied ticks.

DIA_044.name: Acute Hepatitis E
DIA_044.description: Hepatitis E is an inflammation of the liver caused by Hepatitis E virus. Transmission is similar to Hepatitis A, but the viruses are unrelated. First recorded in 1955, but virus was isolated for the first time in 1983.

DIA_045.name: H5N1 Bird Flu

TRM_004.name: Splenopancreatic Laceration

TRM_007.name: Thoracic Gunshot

TRM_024.name: Chemical Burn of the Torso
TRM_024.description: Chemical burn caused by contact with acid, base or oxidizer that can cause extensive tissue damage.

TRM_027.description: Radiation dermatitis, also known as X-ray dermatitis, is a side effect of radiation therapy.

TRM_029.name: Thermal Burn of an Arm
TRM_029.description: Thermal burns are caused by contact with a heated object or a liquid.

TRM_030.description: Thermal burns are caused by contact with a heated object or a liquid.

TRM_032.name: Low Voltage Burn
TRM_032.description: Electrical burn is a result of electricity passing through the body. Unlike visible thermal or chemical burns it causes more sub-dermal damage.

TRM_034.name: Venomous Spider Bite
TRM_034.description: Bites of venomous spiders create tiny red bumps that are sometimes painful and itchy. The injected poison can also cause more severe symptoms.

TRM_035.name: Rattlesnake Bite
TRM_035.description: Snakebites typically have two puncture wounds from the fangs. Identification of the snake species and providing the correct antivenom is an urgent task.

TRM_036.name: Copperhead Snake Bite
TRM_036.description: Snakebites typically have two puncture wounds from the fangs. Identification of the snake species and providing the correct antivenom is an urgent task.

SYM_A1AT_DEFICIENCY.name: A1At Deficiency

SYM_ABDOMINAL_CRAMPS.name: Abdominal Cramps
SYM_ABDOMINAL_CRAMPS.description: Intense cramps in the abdominal area.

SYM_ABDOMINAL_GUARDING.name: Abdominal Guarding
SYM_ABDOMINAL_GUARDING.description: Abdominal guarding, also known as 'défense', is an intense tension of the abdominal wall muscles in response to pressure applied to the abdomen.

SYM_ABDOMINAL_INJURY.name: Abdominal Injury

SYM_ABDOMINAL_PAIN.name: Abdominal Pain

SYM_ABDOMINAL_SWELLING.name: Swollen Abdomen
SYM_ABDOMINAL_SWELLING.description: Enlargement of stomach area, feels uncomfortable and painful.

SYM_ACIDOSIS.name: Acidosis

SYM_AMNESIA_ABOUT_TRAUMA.name: Post-Traumatic Amnesia
SYM_AMNESIA_ABOUT_TRAUMA.description: The patient is disorientated and unable to remember events that occured during or immediately after experiencing a traumatic injury to the brain.

SYM_ANEMIA.name: Anemia

SYM_ANKLE_BRUISE.name: Ankle Bruise

SYM_ANKLE_INJURY.name: Ankle Injury
SYM_ANKLE_INJURY.description: The patient has suffered an injury to their ankle.

SYM_ANXIETY.name: Anxiety
SYM_ANXIETY.description: Anxiety is a normal response to perceived danger, but when its intensity or duration is disproportionate to the actual threat, it becomes abnormal. Pathological anxiety is characterized by persistent tension, worry, and fear without objective justification, often accompanied by various somatic and autonomic nervous system disturbances.

SYM_ASTASIS.name: Astasis
SYM_ASTASIS.description: Inability to stand or walk due to motor incoordination.

SYM_ATHEROSCLEROSIS.name: Atherosclerosis

SYM_AZOTEMIA.name: Azotemia
SYM_AZOTEMIA.description: Abnormal high levels of nitrogen compounds in the blood.

SYM_BACK_INJURY.name: Back Injury
SYM_BACK_INJURY.description: The patient has suffered an injury to their back.

SYM_BACK_PAIN.name: Back Pain
SYM_BACK_PAIN.description: The patient experiences intense pain in the back.

SYM_BACTEREMIA.name: Bacteremia
SYM_BACTEREMIA.description: The presence of bacteria in the blood.

SYM_BAD_BREATH.name: Bad Breath

SYM_BLOATING.name: Bloating

SYM_BLOOD_LOSS.name: Blood Loss
SYM_BLOOD_LOSS.description: Also called hypovolemia, describes a significant loss of blood volume.

SYM_BONE_FRACTURE_VISIBLE.name: Exposed Bone

SYM_BREATHING_DIFFICULTIES.name: Breathing Problems
SYM_BREATHING_DIFFICULTIES.description: The patient has unspecified breathing difficulties.

SYM_BRUISE.name: Bruise
SYM_BRUISE.description: A bruise occurs because of capillary damage due to a trauma

SYM_BURNING_EYE.name: Burning Eye
SYM_BURNING_EYE.description: The patient experiences a burning sensation in their eye.

SYM_BUTTERFLY_RASH.name: Butterfly Rash
SYM_BUTTERFLY_RASH.description: A very unique rash on the face in the shape of a butterfly.

SYM_CARDIOMEGALY.name: Cardiomegaly
SYM_CARDIOMEGALY.description: An abnormal enlargement of the heart.

SYM_CEREBROSPINAL_FLUID_LEAKAGE.name: CSF Leak
SYM_CEREBROSPINAL_FLUID_LEAKAGE.description: The patient has cerebrospinal fluid leaking out of their nose or ear due to a defect in their skull.

SYM_CLOUDY_URINE.description: The urine of the patient appears cloudy.

SYM_CONFUSION.name: Confusion

SYM_CRAMPS.name: Cramps

SYM_CRP_HIGH.description: The patient has raised blood levels of C reactive protein (CRP), a protein produced by the liver in response to infection.

SYM_CRUMBLY_NAIL.name: Crumbly Nail
SYM_CRUMBLY_NAIL.description: The patient has a crumbly looking nail.

SYM_DARK_URINE.description: The urine of the patient appears dark.

SYM_DEGLOVED_SKIN.name: Degloved Skin
SYM_DEGLOVED_SKIN.description: An extensive area of the skin is torn off from the underlying tissue.

SYM_DEHYDRATION.name: Dehydration
SYM_DEHYDRATION.description: The patient has insufficient water in their body.

SYM_DEPRESSION.name: Depression
SYM_DEPRESSION.description: The patient is in a state of despondency and dejection.

SYM_DIARRHEA.name: Diarrhea
SYM_DIARRHEA.description: The patient has frequent bowel discharges with faeces in liquid form.

SYM_EYE_PAIN.description: The patient complains about pain in the eye.

SYM_FAINTING.description: Also called syncope, is a temporary loss of consciousness.

SYM_FEVER.name: Fever

SYM_FINGER_NUMBNESS.name: Finger Numbness
SYM_FINGER_NUMBNESS.description: The patient complains about numbness in one or more fingers.

SYM_FLATULENCE.name: Excessive Flatulence
SYM_FLATULENCE.description: The patient complains about excessive release of gas from their bowels.

SYM_FREQUENT_FALLING.name: Frequent Falling
SYM_FREQUENT_FALLING.description: The patient has a tendency to fall frequently.

SYM_FREQUENT_URINATION.name: Frequent Urination
SYM_FREQUENT_URINATION.description: Passing frequent, small amounts of urine.

SYM_GALLBLADDER_RUPTURE.description: The gallbladder of the patient has ruptured!

SYM_HAIR_LOSS.name: Hair Loss
SYM_HAIR_LOSS.description: Hair loss, also known as alopecia, is a partial or complete absence of hair in body areas that normally have hair.

SYM_HALLUCINATION.name: Hallucinations
SYM_HALLUCINATION.description: An experience of perceptions through the sensory apparatus in the absence of an external stimulus.

SYM_HAND_PAIN.name: Hand Pain
SYM_HAND_PAIN.description: The patient complains about pain in their hand.

SYM_HEAD_TRAUMA.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the head.

SYM_HYPOTENSION.description: Hypotension, also known as low blood pressure, is a condition where the blood flows with a pressure below normal range.

SYM_HYPOVOLEMIC_SHOCK.description: Hypovolemic shock is a life threatening condition due to blood pressure being too low to sustain sufficient blood flow.

SYM_INFLAMMED_SINUSES.description: Inflamed mucous tissue lining that covers the sinuses.

SYM_INJURY_TO_THE_ARM.name: Arm Injury
SYM_INJURY_TO_THE_ARM.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the arm.

SYM_INJURY_TO_THE_COLLARBONE.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the collarbone.

SYM_INJURY_TO_THE_FOOT.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the foot.

SYM_INJURY_TO_THE_HAND.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the hand.

SYM_INJURY_TO_THE_HEAD.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the head.

SYM_INJURY_TO_THE_CHEST.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the chest.

SYM_INJURY_TO_THE_LEG.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the leg.

SYM_INTRACRANIAL_HEMORRHAGE.description: The patient is bleeding within the skull.

SYM_KNEE_INJURY.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the knee.

SYM_NOSEBLEEDS.name: Nosebleed
