SYM_OPEN_WOUND.description: Open wound on the affected area.

SYM_OSTEOPOROSIS.name: Osteoporosis
SYM_OSTEOPOROSIS.description: The condition when bones get gradually weakened, increasing the risk of fracture.

SYM_OTALGIA.name: Otalgia
SYM_OTALGIA.description: Specific ear pain ranging from mild to severe, progressing over 1-2 days.

SYM_PAIN.name: Pain
SYM_PAIN.description: The feeling of physical pain in the affected area.

SYM_PAIN_IN_THE_EARS.name: Pain in the Ears
SYM_PAIN_IN_THE_EARS.description: Unpleasant painful feeling in the ear canal.

SYM_PAINFUL_CERVICAL_LYMPH_NODES.name: Painful Lymph Nodes
SYM_PAINFUL_CERVICAL_LYMPH_NODES.description: Painful feeling caused by swollen cervical lymph nodes.

SYM_PAINFUL_VESICULAR_RASH.name: Painful Vesicular Rash
SYM_PAINFUL_VESICULAR_RASH.description: Painful vesicular skin rash with fluid filled blisters.

SYM_PALE_SKIN.name: Pale Skin
SYM_PALE_SKIN.description: Unusually light skin color compared to its normal tone.

SYM_PETECHIAE.name: Petechiae
SYM_PETECHIAE.description: Tiny purple, red, or brown spots on the skin.

SYM_PHOTOPHOBIA.name: Photophobia
SYM_PHOTOPHOBIA.description: Inability to tolerate light, which causes total discomfort.

SYM_PNEUMATOCELE.name: Pneumatocele
SYM_PNEUMATOCELE.description: An air-filled cavity in lungs, caused by pulmonary laceration.

SYM_POLYURIA.name: Polyuria
SYM_POLYURIA.description: Patient urinates more than usual and passes excessive amounts of urine.

SYM_POOR_BALANCE.name: Poor Balance
SYM_POOR_BALANCE.description: Temporary loss of balance during standing or walking.

SYM_PORTAL_HYPERTENSION.name: Portal Hypertension
SYM_PORTAL_HYPERTENSION.description: High blood pressure in hepatic (liver) portal system.

SYM_PROTEINURIA.name: Proteinuria
SYM_PROTEINURIA.description: Urine analysis confirmed a very high level of proteins in an urine sample.

SYM_PULMONARY_ARTERY_BLOCKAGE.name: Pulmonary Artery Blockage
SYM_PULMONARY_ARTERY_BLOCKAGE.description: Angiography has revealed blockage of patient's pulmonary artery by blood clots.

SYM_RASH.name: Rash
SYM_RASH.description: Rashes may cause the skin to change color, itch, become warm, bumpy, chapped, dry, cracked or blistered, swolen, and may be painful.

SYM_RASH_ON_ARM.name: Rash on Arm
SYM_RASH_ON_ARM.description: Abnormal change of skin color and skin texture distorting the skin's normal appearance, localized on an arm.

SYM_RASH_ON_LEG.name: Rash on Leg
SYM_RASH_ON_LEG.description: Abnormal change of skin color and skin texture distorting the skin's normal appearance, localized on a leg.

SYM_RASH_ON_NECK.name: Rash on Neck
SYM_RASH_ON_NECK.description: Abnormal change of skin color and skin texture distorting the skin's normal appearance, localized on the neck.

SYM_RASH_ON_TORSO.name: Rash on Torso
SYM_RASH_ON_TORSO.description: Abnormal change of skin color and skin texture distorting the skin's normal appearance, localized on the torso.

SYM_RED_EYE.name: Red Eye
SYM_RED_EYE.description: Change of the color of the sclera, from white to red.

SYM_RED_SWOLLEN_TONSILS.name: Red Swollen Tonsils
SYM_RED_SWOLLEN_TONSILS.description: Redness, inflammation and swelling of the tonsils, sometimes severe enough to block the airways.

SYM_REDNESS.name: Redness
SYM_REDNESS.description: Unspecified redness of a certain area or areas on the body.

SYM_REDNESS_OF_SWELLING.name: Redness of a Swelling
SYM_REDNESS_OF_SWELLING.description: Redness of a swollen area caused by inflammation.

SYM_REDNESS_OF_SKIN.name: Redness of Skin
SYM_REDNESS_OF_SKIN.description: Redness of skin caused by an inflammatory process.

SYM_RESPIRATORY_FAILURE.name: Respiratory Failure
SYM_RESPIRATORY_FAILURE.description: Resulting from inadequate gas exchange by the respiratory system.

SYM_RINGING_IN_THE_EARS.name: Tinnitus
SYM_RINGING_IN_THE_EARS.description: Also called ringing in the ears, is the sensation of hearing ringing or other sounds without any external source.

SYM_SEPTIC_SHOCK.name: Septic Shock
SYM_SEPTIC_SHOCK.description: Life-threatening complication of infections that can damage multiple organ systems, causing them to fail.

SYM_SEVERE_ARM_PAIN.name: Severe Arm Pain
SYM_SEVERE_ARM_PAIN.description: Severe arm pain can occur in a wide variety of different injuries or, if accompanied by a pressure in the chest, could be a sign of a heart attack.

SYM_SEVERE_LEG_PAIN.name: Severe Leg Pain
SYM_SEVERE_LEG_PAIN.description: Severe leg pain occurs due to a wide variety of different injuries.

SYM_SEVERE_PAIN_IN_ANKLE.name: Severe Ankle Pain
SYM_SEVERE_PAIN_IN_ANKLE.description: Severe pain in an ankle involving redness around the affected area, but without superficial damage to the skin.

SYM_SHOULDER_INJURY.name: Shoulder Injury
SYM_SHOULDER_INJURY.description: Patient says that they suffered an injury to the shoulder.

SYM_SKIN_AVULSION.name: Skin Avulsion
SYM_SKIN_AVULSION.description: Skin is detached from its normal place on the affected area.

SYM_SKULL_BASIS_FRACTURE.name: Skull Basis Fracture
SYM_SKULL_BASIS_FRACTURE.description: Fracture of a bone in the base of the skull.

SYM_SKULL_FRACTURE.name: Skull Fracture
SYM_SKULL_FRACTURE.description: Fracture of various bones in skull.

SYM_SLEEPING_DIFFICULTIES.name: Sleeping Problems
SYM_SLEEPING_DIFFICULTIES.description: Difficulties in falling asleep, frequent wakeing up during the night, tiredness.

SYM_SLURRED_SPEECH.name: Slurred Speech
SYM_SLURRED_SPEECH.description: Also called dysarthia manifests with a poor pronunciation of words, mumbling, or a change in the speed or rhythm when talking.

SYM_SNEEZING.name: Sneezing
SYM_SNEEZING.description: Convulsive expulsion of air from the lungs through the nose and mouth.

SYM_SORE_THROAT.name: Sore Throat
SYM_SORE_THROAT.description: Includes pain, burning or scratching sensations at the back of the throat, pain when swallowing, and tenderness in the neck.

SYM_SPASMS.name: Spasms
SYM_SPASMS.description: Various kinds of involuntary muscle activity.

SYM_SPUTUM_COUGH.name: Sputum Cough
SYM_SPUTUM_COUGH.description: Productive cough, releasing phlegm from the upper and lower airways.

SYM_STEATORRHEA.name: Steatorrhea
SYM_STEATORRHEA.description: Presence of excess fat in feces. Stool becomes bulkier, pale and foul-smelling.

SYM_STIFFNESS.name: Stiffness
SYM_STIFFNESS.description: Loss of range of motion, associated with pain in the affected area.

SYM_STIFFNESS_IN_THE_HIP.name: Stiffness of the Hip
SYM_STIFFNESS_IN_THE_HIP.description: Loss of range of motion, associated with pain in the hip joint.

SYM_STIFFNESS_IN_THE_KNEE.name: Stiffness of the Knee
SYM_STIFFNESS_IN_THE_KNEE.description: Loss of range of motion, associated with pain in the knee joint.

SYM_STIFFNESS_OF_THE_BACK.name: Stiffness of the Back
SYM_STIFFNESS_OF_THE_BACK.description: Loss of range of motion, associated with pain in the spinal area of back.

SYM_STIFFNESS_OF_THE_JOINTS.name: Stiffness of the Joints
SYM_STIFFNESS_OF_THE_JOINTS.description: Loss of range of motion, associated with pain in the joints.

SYM_STIFFNESS_OF_THE_NECK.name: Stiffness of the Neck
SYM_STIFFNESS_OF_THE_NECK.description: Loss of range of motion, associated with pain in the neck.

SYM_STOMACH_PAIN.name: Stomach Pain
SYM_STOMACH_PAIN.description: Sensation of pain in the stomach could be associated with many diseases. There is a need for further diagnostic processes.

SYM_STRONG-SMELLING_URINE.name: Strong-Smelling Urine
SYM_STRONG-SMELLING_URINE.description: Strong smell of urine could indicate infection in urinary tract but also it could be the results of eating a specific food.

SYM_SUPPURATIVE_TONSILS.name: Suppurative Tonsils
SYM_SUPPURATIVE_TONSILS.description: An inflammation of the parenchyma of the tonsils with tendency to abscess formation. Tonsils appear red and can be enormously swollen.

SYM_SWALLOV_PAIN.name: Swallow Pain
SYM_SWALLOV_PAIN.description: Discomfort while swallowing. Pain is located high in the neck or lower down behind the breastbone. Most often, it feels like a strong sensation of squeezing or burning.

SYM_SWEATING.name: Sweating
SYM_SWEATING.description: Excessive production of fluids secreted by the sweat glands in the skin.

SYM_SWELLING.name: Swelling
SYM_SWELLING.description: Transient abnormal enlargement of an affected area, generally because the tissue is filled with fluid as a reaction to an inflammation or a bruise.

SYM_SWELLING_JOINTS.name: Swelling Joints
SYM_SWELLING_JOINTS.description: Transient abnormal enlargement of a joints, generally because the internal area is filled with fluid.

SYM_SWELLING_KNEE.name: Swelling Knee
SYM_SWELLING_KNEE.description: Transient abnormal enlargement of a knee.

SYM_SWOLLEN_EAR_LOBE.name: Swollen Ear Lobe
SYM_SWOLLEN_EAR_LOBE.description: Enlargement of the ear lobe due to an inflammation or an injury.

SYM_SWOLLEN_EAR_CANAL.name: Swollen Ear Canal
SYM_SWOLLEN_EAR_CANAL.description: Constriction of the ear canal due to the swelling of the lining and tissues.

SYM_SWOLLEN_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_SWOLLEN_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_SWOLLEN_THYROID_GLAND.name: Swollen Thyroid Gland
SYM_SWOLLEN_THYROID_GLAND.description: Abnormal enlargement of the thyroid gland can be a temporary symptom of inflammation of the gland. The inflammatory response generates edema (fluid build up) inside the gland, making it swollen.

SYM_TACHYCARDIA.name: Tachycardia
SYM_TACHYCARDIA.description: Tachycardia (elevated heart rate) is a common symptom mediated by the sympathetic nervous system's flight or fight response and occurs in various diseases or when one is exposed to dangers.

SYM_TENDERNESS.name: Tenderness
SYM_TENDERNESS.description: The affected area is very sensitive when touched.

SYM_THICKENING_AND_DISTORTION_OF_THE_NAI.name: Nail Thickening
SYM_THICKENING_AND_DISTORTION_OF_THE_NAI.description: Thickening and distortion of the patient's nails.

SYM_THUMB_PAIN.name: Thumb Pain
SYM_THUMB_PAIN.description: A burning sensation and pain in the thumb.

SYM_TREMOR.name: Tremor
SYM_TREMOR.description: Unintentional, rhythmic muscle movement involving to-and-from movements (oscillations) of one or more parts of the body (e.g. hands, arms, feet).

SYM_TROUBLE_RISING.name: Trouble Rising
SYM_TROUBLE_RISING.description: Difficuly when attempting to rise from a sitting or prone position.

SYM_ULNAR_CLAW.name: Ulnar Claw
SYM_ULNAR_CLAW.description: Specific and abnormal position of the 4th and 5th fingers (ring and little finger).

SYM_UNCONSCIOUSNESS.name: Unconsciousness
SYM_UNCONSCIOUSNESS.description: State of complete or near-complete lack of responsiveness.

SYM_UNSTOPPABLE_BLEEDING.name: Unstoppable Bleeding
SYM_UNSTOPPABLE_BLEEDING.description: Also called hemorrhaging is a condition in which blood quickly escapes the circulatory system. Unstoppable bleeding can be due to a large wound or due to damage of multiple veins or arteries.

SYM_VISUAL_FIELD_LIMITED.name: Visual Field Limited
SYM_VISUAL_FIELD_LIMITED.description: Perimetry has revealed that the patient has a limited visual field.

SYM_VITAMIN_DEFICIENCY.name: Vitamin Deficiency
SYM_VITAMIN_DEFICIENCY.description: Lack of various vitamines, minerals, or other nutrients in the bloodstream.

SYM_VOMITING.name: Vomiting
SYM_VOMITING.description: Also known as emesis is an involuntary expulsion of stomach contents through the mouth or sometimes through the nose.

SYM_WADDLING_GAIT.name: Waddling Gait
SYM_WADDLING_GAIT.description: Abnormal type of walking, present when the hips are affected by various defects.

SYM_WALKING_DIFFICULTIES.name: Walking Problems
SYM_WALKING_DIFFICULTIES.description: Difficulties with walking straight or maintaining balance.

SYM_WARMTH.name: Warmth
SYM_WARMTH.description: A warm feeling in proximity to the affected area (one of the five signs of inflammation or infection due to an increased blood flow to the area).

SYM_WATERY_EYES.name: Watery Eyes
SYM_WATERY_EYES.description: Watery eyes can be due to a persistent or excessive production of tears or a result of a blocked drainage system from the eye.

SYM_WEAKNESS.name: Weakness
SYM_WEAKNESS.description: Also called asthenia, is a condition where one suffers from extreme temporary or chronic weakness and experiences fatigue, loss of energy, and strength.

SYM_WEIGHT_GAIN.name: Weight Gain
SYM_WEIGHT_GAIN.description: Increase in body weight, usually with no observable or known reason.

SYM_WEIGHT_LOSS.name: Weight Loss
SYM_WEIGHT_LOSS.description: Decrease in body weight, usually with no observable or known reason (is a major symptom in many malignant diseases).

SYM_WRIST_PAIN.name: Wrist Pain
SYM_WRIST_PAIN.description: A tense and burning pain in the wrist.

SYM_CARDIAC_TAMPONADE.name: Cardiac Tamponade
SYM_CARDIAC_TAMPONADE.description: A life-threatening post-surgical condition where the pericardium (a sac surrounding the heart) becomes filled with blood, making it very difficult for the heart to pump.

SYM_BRAIN_SWELLING.name: Brain Swelling
SYM_BRAIN_SWELLING.description: Edema of the brain caused by surgery.

SYM_UNSTABLE_BLOOD_PRESSURE.name: Unstable Blood Pressure
SYM_UNSTABLE_BLOOD_PRESSURE.description: Unstable blood pressure as a reaction to surgery.

SYM_SEIZURES.name: Post-Surgical Seizures
SYM_SEIZURES.description: Short episodes of seizures caused by an abnormal neuronal activity after surgery.

SYM_ABSCESS.name: Abscess
SYM_ABSCESS.description: Buildup of pus within the tissue surrounding a surgical wound.

SYM_ALVEOLAR_COLLAPSE.name: Alveolar Collapse
SYM_ALVEOLAR_COLLAPSE.description: Collapse of the smallest parts of the lung tissue (the alveoli) following sedation and mechanical ventilation during surgery.

SYM_SEPSIS.name: Sepsis
SYM_SEPSIS.description: Post-surgical complication and a life-threatening condition caused by the body's own response to an infection.

SYM_ACL_INJURY.name: ACL Injury
SYM_ACL_INJURY.description: MRI has revealed damage to the anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) in the patient's knee.

SYM_AIRWAYS_INFECTED.name: Airway Infecton
SYM_AIRWAYS_INFECTED.description: Bacterial cultivation of the patient's sputum has revealed the presence of bacteria in the airways.

SYM_ALLERGEN_DETECTED.name: Allergen Detected
SYM_ALLERGEN_DETECTED.description: Skin allergy test has revealed a specific allergen that irritates the patient's skin.

SYM_ALLERGIC_CONJUNCTIVITIS.name: Allergic Conjunctivitis
SYM_ALLERGIC_CONJUNCTIVITIS.description: Skin allergy test has revealed a specific allergen that irritates the patient's eyes.

SYM_ALLERGY_DISCOVERED.name: Allergy Discovered
SYM_ALLERGY_DISCOVERED.description: Skin injection test discovered an increased sensitivity to some allergens.

SYM_ARTERIAL_STENOSIS.name: Arterial Stenosis
SYM_ARTERIAL_STENOSIS.description: Coronary angiography has shown narrowing of the coronary arteries (the arteries supplying the heart with blood).

SYM_ANKLE_CONTUSION.name: Ankle Contusion
SYM_ANKLE_CONTUSION.description: An x-ray showed no broken bones.

SYM_ANKLE_FRACTURE.name: Ankle Fracture
SYM_ANKLE_FRACTURE.description: An x-ray has shown a simple fracture of the ankle.

SYM_ANKLE_SPRAIN.name: Ankle Sprain
SYM_ANKLE_SPRAIN.description: MRI scan of the patient's ankle confirmed that the patient suffered a sprain.

SYM_AORTIC_ANEURYSM.name: Aortic Aneurysm
SYM_AORTIC_ANEURYSM.description: Echocardiography has discovered an aneurysm of the thoracic aorta.

SYM_ARM_PENETRATION.name: Arm Penetration
SYM_ARM_PENETRATION.description: A penetrating trauma to the arm caused a deep wound.

SYM_ASPERGILLOMA_DISCOVERED.name: Aspergilloma Discovered
SYM_ASPERGILLOMA_DISCOVERED.description: CT examination of patient's lungs discovered a large fungal mass - aspergilloma.

SYM_ATOPIC_ECZEMA_FINDINGS.name: Atopic Eczema Findings
SYM_ATOPIC_ECZEMA_FINDINGS.description: Symptoms and findings are suggestive of atopic dermatitis.

SYM_B._ANTHRACIS_DETECTED.name: B. Anthracis Detected
SYM_B._ANTHRACIS_DETECTED.description: Technologists using polymerase chain reaction (PCR) have detected Bacillus anthracis in the patient's blood sample.

SYM_B._HENSELAE_DETECTED.name: B. Henselae Detected
SYM_B._HENSELAE_DETECTED.description: Technologists using polymerase chain reaction (PCR) have detected Bartonella henselae in the patient's blood sample.

SYM_B._PERTUSSIS_DETECTED.name: B. Pertussis Detected
SYM_B._PERTUSSIS_DETECTED.description: Bacterial cultivation from the patient's sputum has identified Bordetella pertussis.

SYM_BABESIOSIS_DETECTED.name: Babesios Detected
SYM_BABESIOSIS_DETECTED.description: Technologists using polymerase chain reaction (PCR) have detected presence of Babesia infection in a sample of the patient's peripheral blood.

SYM_BACTERIAL_BLEPHARITIS.name: Bacterial Blepharitis
SYM_BACTERIAL_BLEPHARITIS.description: Cultivation of the swab taken from patient's skin confirms the presence of a bacterial infection.

SYM_BACTERIAL_CONJUNCTIVITIS.name: Bacterial Conjunctivitis
SYM_BACTERIAL_CONJUNCTIVITIS.description: Cultivation of a sample taken from the patient's eye discharge confirmed the presence of a bacterial infection.

SYM_BEEF_TAPEWORM_PRESENT.name: Beef Tapeworm Detected
SYM_BEEF_TAPEWORM_PRESENT.description: Beef tapeworm has been detected in the patient's stool sample.

SYM_BLADDER_INFECTION.name: Bladder Infection
SYM_BLADDER_INFECTION.description: A bacterial infection of the bladder has been identified.

SYM_BLADDER_LINING_DEFECTED.name: Bladder Lining Defect
SYM_BLADDER_LINING_DEFECTED.description: Cystography has found various defects in the lining of the bladder.

SYM_BLADDER_STONES_DETECTED.name: Bladder Stones Detected
SYM_BLADDER_STONES_DETECTED.description: Ultrasonography has shown multiple stones in the patient's bladder.

SYM_BLUNT_KIDNEY_CONTUSION.name: Blunt Kidney Contusion
SYM_BLUNT_KIDNEY_CONTUSION.description: A CT scan has revealed a contusion of the patient's kidney tissue.

SYM_BLUNT_PANCREAS_CONTUSION.name: Blunt Pancreas Contusion
SYM_BLUNT_PANCREAS_CONTUSION.description: Ultrasonography has revealed a minor pancreatic contusion.

SYM_BLUNT_PANCREAS_LACERATION.name: Blunt Pancreas Laceration
SYM_BLUNT_PANCREAS_LACERATION.description: Ultrasonography has revealed a laceration in the patient's pancreatic tissue.

SYM_BLUNT_PULMONARY_CONTUSION.name: Blunt Pulmonary Contusion
SYM_BLUNT_PULMONARY_CONTUSION.description: A CT scan has revealed a pulmonary contusion.

SYM_BLUNT_SPLEEN_CONTUSION.name: Blunt Spleen Contusion
SYM_BLUNT_SPLEEN_CONTUSION.description: A CT scan has revealed a spleen contusion.

SYM_BLUNT_SPLEEN_RUPTURE.name: Blunt Spleen Rupture
SYM_BLUNT_SPLEEN_RUPTURE.description: A CT scan has revealed a splenic rupture, which occured due to blunt trauma.

SYM_BONE_INFECTED.name: Bone Infection
SYM_BONE_INFECTED.description: MRI of the patient's bone tissue has revealed an infection.

SYM_BRAIN_HEMATOMAS.name: Brain Hematomas
SYM_BRAIN_HEMATOMAS.description: A CT scan has discovered both an epidural and an intracerebral hematoma.

SYM_BRAIN_INFLAMMATION.name: Brain Inflammation
SYM_BRAIN_INFLAMMATION.description: Spinal fluid analysis has detected a high count of white blood cells that is suggestive of an inflammation in the brain.

SYM_BRONCHIAL_HYPERREACTIVITY.name: Bronchial Hyperreactivity
SYM_BRONCHIAL_HYPERREACTIVITY.description: A special bronchial test indicates that the patient suffers from asthma.

SYM_BRONCHIAL_INFECTION.name: Bronchial Infection
SYM_BRONCHIAL_INFECTION.description: Bacterial cultivation of the patient's expectorate (the slime one can cough up) has proven the presence of bacteria in the bronchi.

SYM_CALF_CONTUSION.name: Calf Contusion
SYM_CALF_CONTUSION.description: An x-ray examination did not show any broken bones in the patient's calf.

SYM_CARBOXYHEMOGLOBIN_HIGH_LEVEL.name: COHb High Level
SYM_CARBOXYHEMOGLOBIN_HIGH_LEVEL.description: A blood test has revealed high levels of carboxyhemoglobin in the patient's blood, suggestive of carbon monoxide poisoning.

SYM_CAROTID_ARTERIES_BLOCKED.name: Carotid Arteries Blocked
SYM_CAROTID_ARTERIES_BLOCKED.description: Ultrasonography with Doppler-techqniue has revealed a blockage of the carotid arteries due blood clots.

SYM_CELIAC_ANTIBODIES_DETECTED.name: Celiac Antibodies Detected
SYM_CELIAC_ANTIBODIES_DETECTED.description: A serology test identified specific celiac antibodies.

SYM_CEREBRAL_CONTUSION_FINDINGS.name: Cerebral Contusion
SYM_CEREBRAL_CONTUSION_FINDINGS.description: A CT scan of the patient's brain has revealed cerebral contusion.

SYM_CHR._FATIGUE_SYNDROME.name: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
SYM_CHR._FATIGUE_SYNDROME.description: Evaluation of all the patient's symptoms is suggestive of Chronic Fatigue Syndrome.

SYM_CLAVICLE_FRACTURE.name: Clavicle Fracture
SYM_CLAVICLE_FRACTURE.description: A CT scan has shown a simple fracture of the clavicle.

SYM_CLAVICLE_SHAFT_FRACTURE.name: Clavicle Shaft Fracture
SYM_CLAVICLE_SHAFT_FRACTURE.description: A CT scan has shown a fracture of the shaft of the clavicle.

SYM_CLOSED_ANGLE.name: Closed Angle
SYM_CLOSED_ANGLE.description: Gonioscopic examination has revealed a closed angle between the iris and cornea, suggestive of closed angle glaucoma.

SYM_CLOTTING_FACTOR_LOW.name: Clotting Factor Low
SYM_CLOTTING_FACTOR_LOW.description: Blood tests have detected low levels of clotting factor in the patient's blood sample.

SYM_CLUSTER_HEADACHE_FINDINGS.name: Cluster Headache
SYM_CLUSTER_HEADACHE_FINDINGS.description: MRI findings support the diagnosis of cluster headache disorder.

SYM_CONCUSSION.name: Concussion
SYM_CONCUSSION.description: A CT scan of the patient's brain is suggestive of a concussion.

SYM_CONSTANT_BRONCHIAL_INFECTION.name: Bronchial Infection - Chronic
SYM_CONSTANT_BRONCHIAL_INFECTION.description: X-ray findings are suggestive of constant irritation of the bronchi due to a widespread infection.

SYM_CROHN_DISEASE_DISCOVERED.name: Crohn's Disease Discovered
SYM_CROHN_DISEASE_DISCOVERED.description: CT enterography has revealed findings suggestive of Crohn's disease.

SYM_CYSTIC_DUCT_OBSTRUCTED.name: Cystic Duct Obstructed
SYM_CYSTIC_DUCT_OBSTRUCTED.description: Ultrasonography has revealed an obstruction of the cystic duct due to a large gallstone.

SYM_DEGENERATIVE_FINDINGS.name: Degenerative Findings
SYM_DEGENERATIVE_FINDINGS.description: MRI has revealed degenerative changes in the patient's cervical spine.

SYM_DEMYELINIZATION_FINDINGS.name: Demyelinization Findings
SYM_DEMYELINIZATION_FINDINGS.description: Biopsy of the sural nerve with consequent analysis has found signs of demyelinization.

SYM_DEPICTION_OF_FEMUR_FRACTURE.name: Fractured Femur Depicted
SYM_DEPICTION_OF_FEMUR_FRACTURE.description: The x-ray shows an open fracture of the femur.

SYM_DEPICTION_OF_HUMERUS_FRACTURE.name: Fractured Humerus Depicted
SYM_DEPICTION_OF_HUMERUS_FRACTURE.description: The x-ray shows of an open fracture of the humerus.

SYM_DEPICTION_OF_TIBIA_FRACTURE.name: Fractured Tibia Depicted
SYM_DEPICTION_OF_TIBIA_FRACTURE.description: The x-ray shows an open fracture of the tibia.

SYM_DEPICTION_OF_ULNAR_FRACTURE.name: Fractured Ulna Depicted
SYM_DEPICTION_OF_ULNAR_FRACTURE.description: The x-ray shows an open fracture of the ulna.

SYM_DIFFUSE_AXONAL_INJURY.name: Diffuse Axonal Injury
SYM_DIFFUSE_AXONAL_INJURY.description: MRI scan has revealed multiple findings that are suggestive of diffuse axonal injury.

SYM_DILATED_VENTRICLE.name: Dilated Ventricle
SYM_DILATED_VENTRICLE.description: Angiography of the patient's heart shows a dilated left ventricle.

SYM_DUODENITIS_DISCOVERED.name: Duodenitis Discovered
SYM_DUODENITIS_DISCOVERED.description: Gastroscopy has revealed inflammation of the duodenum.

SYM_E._HISTOLYTICA_DISCOVERED.name: E. Histolytica Discovered
SYM_E._HISTOLYTICA_DISCOVERED.description: Bacterial cultivation has detected Entamoeba histolytica in the patient's stool sample.

SYM_EAR_CANAL_INFECTED.name: Ear Canal Infected
SYM_EAR_CANAL_INFECTED.description: Examination of the outer ear has revealed an infection of the outer ear canal.

SYM_EBV_DETECTED.name: EBV Detected
SYM_EBV_DETECTED.description: Serology testing detected specific Epstein-Barr virus antigens.

SYM_ELBOW_MUSCLES_DAMAGED.name: Elbow Muscles Damaged
SYM_ELBOW_MUSCLES_DAMAGED.description: MRI has revealed signs of inflammation the elbow's muscles, most likely due to overuse.

SYM_ELBOW_TENDONS_DAMAGED.name: Elbow Tendons Damaged
SYM_ELBOW_TENDONS_DAMAGED.description: X-ray has discovered damaged tendons of the muscles of the elbow.

SYM_EMPHYSEMA_DISCOVERED.name: Emphysema Discovered

SYM_HYPOTHERMIA.name: Hypothermia
SYM_HYPOTHERMIA.description: Hypothermia refers to a condition when body core temperature drops significantly.

SYM_FOREIGN_OBJECT_PRESENT.name: Foreign Object Present
SYM_FOREIGN_OBJECT_PRESENT.description: Foreign object or its parts are still present in the wound and may lead to serious complications.

SYM_LIVER_DAMAGED.name: Liver Damaged
SYM_LIVER_DAMAGED.description: Serious internal damage to the liver may cause life-threatening complications.

SYM_SPLEEN_DAMAGED.name: Spleen Damaged
SYM_SPLEEN_DAMAGED.description: Serious internal damage to the spleen may cause life-threatening complications.

SYM_LUNGS_PERFORATED.name: Lungs Perforated
SYM_LUNGS_PERFORATED.description: Perforation of the lung membranes and tissue.

SYM_PANCREAS_DAMAGED.name: Pancreas Damaged
SYM_PANCREAS_DAMAGED.description: Serious internal damage to the pancreatic tissue may cause life-threatening complications.

SYM_KIDNEY_DAMAGED.name: Kidney Damaged
SYM_KIDNEY_DAMAGED.description: Serious internal damage to the kidney tissue may cause life-threatening complications.

SYM_STOMACH_PERFORATED.name: Stomach Perforated
SYM_STOMACH_PERFORATED.description: Perforation of the stomach may cause life-threatening complications.

SYM_SMALL_INTESTINE_PERFORATED.name: Small Intestine Perforated
SYM_SMALL_INTESTINE_PERFORATED.description: Serious perforation of the small intestine may cause life-threatening complications.

SYM_LARGE_INTESTINE_PERFORATED.name: Large Intestine Perforated
SYM_LARGE_INTESTINE_PERFORATED.description: Serious perforation of the large intestine may cause life-threatening complications.

SYM_TIBIAL_FRACTURE.name: Tibial Fracture
SYM_TIBIAL_FRACTURE.description: Broken tibial bone - major calf bone.

SYM_ULNAR_FRACTURE.name: Ulnar Fracture
SYM_ULNAR_FRACTURE.description: Broken ulnar bone - one of the two bones located in a forearm.

SYM_RADIAL_FRACTURE.name: Radial Fracture
SYM_RADIAL_FRACTURE.description: Broken radial bone - one of the two bones located in a forearm.

SYM_HUMERAL_FRACTURE.name: Humeral Fracture
SYM_HUMERAL_FRACTURE.description: Broken humerus - located in shoulder part of arm.

SYM_BLISTERS.name: Blisters
SYM_BLISTERS.description: A small pocket of fluid in the space between upper layer of the skin (epidermis) and layers beneath, most often caused by skin being damaged by friction or heat.

SYM_DESQUAMATION.name: Desquamation
SYM_DESQUAMATION.description: Also known as peeling skin is shedding of the outermost layer of the skin.

SYM_NECROSIS.name: Necrosis
SYM_NECROSIS.description: Premature death of a tissue caused by its own enzymes. It occurs when too little blood flows to the tissue. This can be cause by an injury, radiation, or chemicals. Necrosis cannot be reversed.

SYM_GUNSHOT_WOUND.name: Gunshot Wound
SYM_GUNSHOT_WOUND.description: Entry or entry-and-exit wound caused by a projectile shot from a firearm.

SYM_BALLISTIC_WOUND.name: Ballistic Wound
SYM_BALLISTIC_WOUND.description: Entry-and-exit or an entry wound caused by a projectile.

SYM_PENETRATING_WOUND.name: Penetrating Wound
SYM_PENETRATING_WOUND.description: Wound caused by penetration of a foreign object.

SYM_BLAST_WOUND.name: Blast Wound
SYM_BLAST_WOUND.description: Wound or wounds caused by an explosion.

SYM_BURN_WOUND.name: Burn Wound
SYM_BURN_WOUND.description: A burn wound caused by heat, cold, electricity, chemicals, friction, or radiation, affects skin or other tissues.

EXM_ABDOMINAL_PALPATION.name: Abdominal Palpation
EXM_ABDOMINAL_PALPATION.description: Type of physical examination. The examiner palpates (touches) nine areas of the patient's abdomen, performing it twice, both superficially and deeply. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_ANGIOGRAPHY.name: Angiography
EXM_ANGIOGRAPHY.description: An imaging method used to visualize the lumen of the blood vessels and organs, particulary the arteries, the veins, and the heart. \n\nRequired room: CAG room

EXM_AUDIOMETRY.name: Audiometry
EXM_AUDIOMETRY.description: Audiometric testing determines patient's level of hearing. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at Emergency / ICU / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / DID

EXM_BACTERIA_CULTIVATION_SAMPLING.name: Microbial Sampling
EXM_BACTERIA_CULTIVATION_SAMPLING.description:
Microbial sampling is the collection of samples from the patient (e.g. body fluids, sputum, stool) for further analysis. 

Required room: Microbiology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt / ICU 

Required equipment: Glassware + Fume hood + Microbiolog. incubator + Microscope

EXM_BACTERIA_CULTIVATION_TESTING.name: Microbial Cultivation
EXM_BACTERIA_CULTIVATION_TESTING.description: Laboratory cultivation of the specific sample obtained from the patient in order to determine if bacterias are present and which kind of bacteria it is. \n\nRequired room: Microbiology Lab

EXM_BARRIUM_SWALLOV_X_RAY.name: Barium Swallow
EXM_BARRIUM_SWALLOV_X_RAY.description: A test where the patient swallows a barium sulfate mixture that shows up on x-ray. It is used to examine the lumen of the esophagus, stomach, and intestines. \n\nRequired room: X-ray room

EXM_BASIC_VISUAL_TEST.name: Basic Visual Test
EXM_BASIC_VISUAL_TEST.description:
Common visual test used to examine the patient's visual acuity. 

Required room: Any office at Emergency / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology / Neurology / DID 

Required equipment: Eye test

EXM_BIOPSY_SAMPLING.name: Biopsy - Sampling
EXM_BIOPSY_SAMPLING.description:
A surgical procedure where a piece of tissue (e.g. lung, liver, intestine) is taken out for further testing. 

Required room: Histology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Orthopaedy dpt / Cardiology dpt / Neurology dpt / ICU 

Required equipment: Glassware + Lab analyzer + Microscope

EXM_BIOPSY_TESTING.name: Biopsy - Testing
EXM_BIOPSY_TESTING.description: The piece of tissue that was taken out during the biopsy procedure is examined under a microscope and treated with various preparations to allow for more specific testing. \n\nRequired room: Histology Lab

EXM_BLOOD_PRESSURE_AND_PULSE_MEASUREMENT.name: Blood Pressure Measurement
EXM_BLOOD_PRESSURE_AND_PULSE_MEASUREMENT.description: The classic, manual measurement of the patient's blood pressure and pulse (heartbeat frequency) using sphygmomanometer. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_BLOOD_TEST_SAMPLING.name: Blood Draw
EXM_BLOOD_TEST_SAMPLING.description:
The collection of a sample of the patient's blood (venous or arterial) for further analysis or testing. 

Required room: Hematology Lab 

Sampling: Any department 

Required equipment: Glassware + Hematoanalyzer

EXM_BLOOD_TEST_TESTING.name: Blood Test
EXM_BLOOD_TEST_TESTING.description: Laboratory analysis of a blood sample. \n\nRequired room: Hematology Lab

EXM_CBC_SAMPLING.name: CBC - Sampling
EXM_CBC_SAMPLING.description:
The sampling of blood for a Complete Blood Count (CBC) test. 

Required room: Hematology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Orthopaedy dpt / Neurology dpt / ICU / DID / Traumatology dpt 

Required equipment: Glassware + Hematoanalyzer

EXM_CBC_TESTING.name: CBC - Testing
EXM_CBC_TESTING.description: Complete Blood Count (CBC) test counts all of the cells found in the blood and determines if there is an increase or decrease in any of those cells. \n\nRequired room: Hematology Lab

EXM_CC_BLOOD_ANALYSIS.name: Blood Analysis - ICU
EXM_CC_BLOOD_ANALYSIS.description:
Special blood analysis that is mostly used at intensive care units. 

Required room: ICU room 

Required equipment: Hematoanalyzer

EXM_CC_ECHO.name: Urgent Echo
EXM_CC_ECHO.description:
Special critical care echocardiography used mainly in intensive care units or trauma centers to assess if the heart is pumping properly, if there is any fluid in the pericardium (the sac around the heart) or fluid surrounding the lungs. 

Required room: ICU room / Trauma center 

Required equipment: FAST usg

EXM_HEART_MONITORING.name: Heart Monitoring
EXM_HEART_MONITORING.description:
Longterm monitoring of the heart's eletrical activity. 

Required room: ICU / Trauma center / Observation room 

Requiered equipment: Wall monitor / Anesthetic machine

EXM_CRP.name: CRP
EXM_CRP.description: C-reactive protein (CRP) is diagnostic test based on a quick blood test measuring the concentration of C-reactive protein that is indicative of inflammatory and infectious diseases. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU at Any department except Neurology dpt

EXM_CT.name: CT
EXM_CT.description: Computed tomography (CT) is an imaging modality that creates detailed images of the internal organs and bones. By injecting intravenous contrast or making the patient swallow contrast, the use of CT can be used to assess a wider range of diseases. \n\nRequired room: CT room

EXM_CT_ENTEROGRAPHY.name: CT - Enterography
EXM_CT_ENTEROGRAPHY.description: Computed tomography (CT) enterography is diagnostic imaging modality creating detailed images of the intestines by injecting intravenous contrast after the patient has ingested liquid contrast. \n\nRequired room: CT room

EXM_ECG.name: ECG
EXM_ECG.description: Electrocardiography (ECG) is the recording of the heart's electrical activity by electrodes on the skin. \n\nRequired room: Cardiography unit

EXM_EEG.name: EEG
EXM_EEG.description: Electroencephalography (EEG) is the recording of the brain's electrical activity by equipping the patient with a hat containing many electrodes. \n\nRequired room: Neuroexam unit

EXM_ECHO.name: Echo
EXM_ECHO.description: Echocardiography is the ultrasonographic assessment of the patient's heart and large veins and arteries entering and leaving the heart. \n\nRequired room: Cardiography unit

EXM_EMG.name: EMG
EXM_EMG.description: Electromyography (EMG) is the recording and evaluation of the electrical activity of the patient's muscles. \n\nRequired room: Office/Diagnostic unit at Orthopaedy dpt

EXM_EVALUATION.name: Evaluation
EXM_EVALUATION.description: Considering all the symptoms and examinations recently carried out to specify the diagnosis. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_FAST.name: FAST
EXM_FAST.description: Fast Assessment with Sonography in Trauma (FAST) is a special ultrasonography method used in the evaluation of trauma patients and intensive care unit patients. \n\nRequired room: Trauma center, ICU

EXM_FECAL_ELASTASE_TEST_SAMPLING.name: Elastase Test - Sampling
EXM_FECAL_ELASTASE_TEST_SAMPLING.description:
The collection of a stool sample from the patient in order to quantify the amount of the elastase produced by the pancreas. It indicates the actual function of the pancreas. 

Required room: Hematology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / ICU 

Required equipment: Glassware + RIA

EXM_FECAL_ELASTASE_TEST_TESTING.name: Elastase Test - Testing
EXM_FECAL_ELASTASE_TEST_TESTING.description: The measurement of elastase in a stool sample from a patient. The amount indicates the actual function of the pancreas. \n\nRequired room: Hematology Lab

EXM_FUNGAL_CULTIVATION_SAMPLING.name: Mycologic Sampling
EXM_FUNGAL_CULTIVATION_SAMPLING.description:
Collecting various samples from the patient's body for cultivation in order to determine if fungal species are present or not. 

Required room: Microbiology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / ICU 

Required equipment: Glassware + Fume hood + Microbiolog. incubator + Microscope

EXM_FUNGAL_CULTIVATION_TESTING.name: Fungal Cultivation
EXM_FUNGAL_CULTIVATION_TESTING.description: Laboratory cultivation of the specific samples obtained from the patient's body. \n\nRequired room: Microbiology Lab

EXM_GASTROSCOPY.name: Gastroscopy
EXM_GASTROSCOPY.description: A procedure where a flexible tube (endoscope) with a tiny video camera and a light source at the end is inserted through the mouth in order to examine the upper part of digestive tract.\n\nRequired room: ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology dpt / DID

EXM_GONIOSCOPY.name: Gonioscopy
EXM_GONIOSCOPY.description: Gonioscopy is an eye examination used to evaluate the drainage system of the eye. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at Neurology

EXM_CHEST_LISTENING.name: Chest Auscultation
EXM_CHEST_LISTENING.description: The auscultation (listening with a stethoscope) of the heart and lungs. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_LARYNGOSCOPY.name: Laryngoscopy
EXM_LARYNGOSCOPY.description: A procedure where a flexible tube (endoscope) with a tiny video camera and a light source in the end is inserted through the nose to examine the larynx, glottis, and vocal chords. Laryngoscopy is also used to facilitate tracheal intubation. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU

EXM_MRI.name: MRI
EXM_MRI.description: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) creates detailed images of the anatomy and physiology of the body and internal organs by using a magnetic fields, magnetics waves, and radio waves. \n\nRequired room: MRI room

EXM_NASAL_CAVITY_INSPECTION.name: Nasal Cavity Inspection
EXM_NASAL_CAVITY_INSPECTION.description: Examination and visual inspection of the patient's nose including the nasal cavity. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_NECK_PALPATION.name: Neck Palpation
EXM_NECK_PALPATION.description: Palpation (touching) of the patient's neck. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_NEUROLOGICAL_TESTING.name: Neurological Testing
EXM_NEUROLOGICAL_TESTING.description: Different simple and specific questions and tests that provide information about the patient's central nervous system. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit at all departments except Orthopaedy dpt

EXM_OPHTHALMOSCOPY.name: Ophthalmoscopy
EXM_OPHTHALMOSCOPY.description: A procedure where the fundus/retina is visualised through a funduscope/opthalmoscope. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Emergency / ICU / Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt / Traumatology dpt

EXM_ORAL_CAVITY_INSPECTION.name: Oral Cavity Inspection
EXM_ORAL_CAVITY_INSPECTION.description: Examination and visual inspection of the patient's oral cavity. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit at all departments except Orthopaedy dpt and Neurology

EXM_PCR_SAMPLING.name: PCR - Sampling
EXM_PCR_SAMPLING.description:
Sampling of biological material for polymerase chain reaction analysis in the laboratory. Examination is quick, cheap and exact. 

Required room: Hematology Lab 

Sampling: All departments 

Required equipment: Glassware + Thermal cycler

EXM_PCR_TESTING.name: PCR - Testing
EXM_PCR_TESTING.description: Polymerase chain reaction is quick examination method of identifying infectious disease caused by bacteria or viruses. Examination is quick, cheap and exact. \n\nRequired room: Hematology Lab

EXM_PERIMETRY.name: Perimetry
EXM_PERIMETRY.description: Perimetry is ophtalmology test that systematically tests the complete field of vision. \n\nRequired room: Neuroexam Unit at Neurology dpt

EXM_PERITONEAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.name: Peritoneal Fluid Analysis - Sampling
EXM_PERITONEAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.description:
Sampling of peritoneal fluid with a large needle and syringe through the abdominal wall. 

Required room: Histology Lab 

Sampling: General Surgery dpt 

Required equipment: Glassware + Medical centrifuge + Lab analyzer

EXM_PERITONEAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_TESTING.name: Peritoneal Fluid Analysis - Testing
EXM_PERITONEAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_TESTING.description: Biochemical test that analyzes the fluid collected from the peritoneal space. \n\nRequired room: Histology Lab

EXM_PHYSICAL_AND_VISUAL_EXAMINATION.name: Physical Examination
EXM_PHYSICAL_AND_VISUAL_EXAMINATION.description: Examination of the specific area on the patient's body. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_INTERVIEW.name: Interview
EXM_INTERVIEW.description: Interviewing the patient to get as much information as possible about their medical condition. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_SEROLOGY_TESTING_SAMPLING.name: Serologic Sampling
EXM_SEROLOGY_TESTING_SAMPLING.description:
Drawing of a blood sample for serologic tests. 

Required room: Hematology Lab 

Sampling: All departments 

Required equipment: Glassware + RIA

EXM_SEROLOGY_TESTING_TESTING.name: Serologic Testing
EXM_SEROLOGY_TESTING_TESTING.description: Special blood test examining specific antibodies and antigens. Antibodies are proteins made by human immune system to neutralize pathogens. \n\nRequired room: Hematology Lab

EXM_SKIN_INJECTION_TEST.name: Skin Allergy Test
EXM_SKIN_INJECTION_TEST.description: Injection of a small amount of different allergen extracts into the skin usually on the arm to confirm or deny the allergic reactions to specific allergens. \n\nRequired room: Offices / Diagnostic units at Emergency dpt / ICU dpt / Neurology dpt

EXM_SPEECH_LISTENING.name: Speech Listening
EXM_SPEECH_LISTENING.description: Listening to speech flow of the patient. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_SPINAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.name: CSF Sampling
EXM_SPINAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.description:
Sampling of cerebrospinal fluid is performed by inserting a needle through the lower back into the spinal canal, draining a small amount of the fluid. 

Required room: Histology Lab 

Sampling: Neurology dpt / DID 

Required equipment: Glassware + Medical centrifuge + Lab analyzer

EXM_SPINAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_TESTING.name: CSF Analysis
EXM_SPINAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_TESTING.description: Process of analysis of cerebrospinal fluid in the laboratory. \n\nRequired room: Histology Lab

EXM_SPIROMETRY.name: Spirometry
EXM_SPIROMETRY.description: One of the most common pulmonary function test, measuring lung function. \n\nRequired room: Office / Diagnostic Unit at Internal Medicine dpt

EXM_STOOL_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.name: Stool Collecting
EXM_STOOL_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.description:
Procedure to collect a sample of patient's stool for further analysis. 

Required room: Histology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / ICU / DID 

Required equipment: Glassware + Stool analyzer

EXM_STOOL_ANALYSIS_TESTING.name: Stool Analysis
EXM_STOOL_ANALYSIS_TESTING.description: Analysis of the patient's stool for bacteria, blood, parasites etc. \n\nRequired room: Histology Lab

EXM_SWEAT_CHLORIDE_TEST.name: Sweat Chloride Test
EXM_SWEAT_CHLORIDE_TEST.description: Special biochemical test used to diagnose cystic fibrosis. \n\nRequired room: Office / Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt / ICU

EXM_TEMPERATURE_MEASUREMENT.name: Temperature Measurement
EXM_TEMPERATURE_MEASUREMENT.description: Examination to determine the current body temperature. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_THORAX_PERCUSSION.name: Thorax Percussion
EXM_THORAX_PERCUSSION.description: Examination based on systematically tapping on the thorax to identify areas with less air within the thorax. \n\nRequired room: Any office/Diagnostic unit

EXM_TONOMETRY.name: Tonometry
EXM_TONOMETRY.description: Test that measures pressure inside the eye also known as intraocular pressure. \n\nRequired room: Neuroexam unit

EXM_TRANSIENT_ELASTOGRAPHY.name: Transient Elastography
EXM_TRANSIENT_ELASTOGRAPHY.description: Special ultra sound imaging method that maps elastic properties and stiffness of soft tissues in the internal organs. \n\nRequired room: Sonography unit at General Surgery dpt / Radiology

EXM_URGENT_BLOOD_ANALYSIS.name: PS Blood Control
EXM_URGENT_BLOOD_ANALYSIS.description:
Post surgery blood analysis is used in post-operative care in patients that could suffer from sepsis. 

Required room: Cardiography unit 

Required equipment: Lab analyzer

EXM_URINE_SAMPLE_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.name: Urine Analysis - Sampling
EXM_URINE_SAMPLE_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.description:
Sampling of mid-stream urine from a patient, or from a patient's catheter. 

Required room: Histology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology dpt / ICU / DID / Traumatology dpt 

Required equipment: Glassware + Lab analyzer

EXM_URINE_SAMPLE_ANALYSIS_TESTING.name: Urine Analysis - Testing
EXM_URINE_SAMPLE_ANALYSIS_TESTING.description: Biochemical analysis of a urine sample. \n\nRequired room: Histology Lab

EXM_USG.name: USG
EXM_USG.description: Ultrasonography is a medical imaging technique using ultrasound waves, resulting in visualization of both soft and hard tissues and organs. \n\nRequired room: Sonography unit at General Surgery dpt / Orthopaedy dpt / Cardiology / Radiology

EXM_X_RAY_BACK.name: X-Ray Back
EXM_X_RAY_BACK.description: X-ray imaging of the bones of the back. \n\nRequired room: X-ray room

EXM_X_RAY_HEAD.name: X-Ray Head
EXM_X_RAY_HEAD.description: X-ray imaging of the bones of the skull. \n\nRequired room: X-ray room

EXM_X_RAY_LOWER_LIMB.name: X-Ray Lower Limb
EXM_X_RAY_LOWER_LIMB.description: X-ray imaging of the bones of the lower limb. \n\nRequired room: X-ray room

EXM_X_RAY_TORSO.name: X-Ray Chest
EXM_X_RAY_TORSO.description: X-ray imaging of the bones and shadows of the thorax. \n\nRequired room: X-ray room

EXM_X_RAY_UPPER_LIMB.name: X-Ray Upper Limb
EXM_X_RAY_UPPER_LIMB.description: X-ray imaging of the bones the arm. \n\nRequired room: X-ray room

EXM_DIFFERENTIAL_DIAGNOSIS.name: Differential Diagnosis
EXM_DIFFERENTIAL_DIAGNOSIS.description: Special case-review that reduces the amount of possible diagnoses. Can only be performed by a doctor with advanced diagnosis specialization.

EXM_RT_PCR_SAMPLING.name: RT-PCR Sampling
EXM_RT_PCR_SAMPLING.description:
Reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction is a laboratory technique combining reverse transcription of RNA into DNA and amplification of specific DNA targets using PCR. 

Required room: Hematology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / Infectious diseases dpt / ICU /

Required equipment: Glassware + Thermal cycler

EXM_RT_PCR_TESTING.name: RT-PCR Testing
EXM_RT_PCR_TESTING.description: Reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction is a laboratory technique combining reverse transcription of RNA into DNA and amplification of specific DNA targets using PCR. \n\nRequired room: Hematology Lab

EXM_ELISA_SAMPLING.name: ELISA Sampling
EXM_ELISA_SAMPLING.description:
Enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay is a laboratory technique used to detect and quantify peptides, proteins, antibodies and hormones. 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Infectious diseases dpt / ICU 

Required equipment: Glassware + RIA

EXM_ELISA_TESTING.name: ELISA Testing
EXM_ELISA_TESTING.description: Enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay is a laboratory technique used to detect and quantify peptides, proteins, antibodies and hormones. \n\nRequired room: Hematology Lab

EXM_PULSE_OXYMETRY.name: Pulse Oxymetry
EXM_PULSE_OXYMETRY.description: Pulse oxymetry is an easy-to-use and effective method for monitoring patient's oxygen saturation.

EXM_RECEPTION_FAST.name: Triage in the Reception
EXM_RECEPTION_FAST.description: Receptionists can assign a higher priority to the patient based on their symptoms. \n\nRequired room: Reception

TRT_HOME_TREATMENT.name: Home Treatment
TRT_HOME_TREATMENT.description: Patient will treat himself at home. No hospitalization is required. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

TRT_A1AT_REPLACEMENT.name: A1At Replacement
TRT_A1AT_REPLACEMENT.description: Replacement of the protein Alpha-1 antitrypsin by the intravenous route. \n\nRequired room: Offices / Diagnostic units at General Surgery dpt, Internal Medicine dpt / ICU dpt / Regular wards / High dependency units

TRT_ACE_INHIBITOR.name: ACE Inhibitor
TRT_ACE_INHIBITOR.description: A drug used in the treatment of hypertension. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit at all departments except Emergency and Infectious diseases dpt

TRT_ANALGESICS.name: Analgesics
TRT_ANALGESICS.description: Drugs, mostly in pill form, used to relieve pain. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

TRT_ANTACIDS.name: Antacids
TRT_ANTACIDS.description: Antacids are substances that neutralize stomach acid, used to relieve heartburn, indigestion or reflux. \n\nRequired room: Office / Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt

TRT_ANTI_SEIZURES_MEDICINE.name: Anti Seizures Medicine
TRT_ANTI_SEIZURES_MEDICINE.description: Medicine used to treat various types and forms of seizures. \n\nRequired room: Any diagnostic unit / ICU

TRT_ANTI_ARRHYTMICALS.name: Antiarrhythmics
TRT_ANTI_ARRHYTMICALS.description: Drugs usually given intravenously or as pills to suppress abnormal rhythms of the heart. \n\nRequired room: ICU /  Any office / Diagnostic unit at Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt / Traumatology dpt

TRT_ANTIBIOTICS.name: Antibiotics
TRT_ANTIBIOTICS.description: Drugs in various forms used to treat various bacterial infections. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

TRT_ANTIBIOTIC_OINTMENT.name: Antibiotic Ointment
TRT_ANTIBIOTIC_OINTMENT.description: Special ointment applied to infected areas on the skin. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Office/Diagnostic unit at Neurology dpt

TRT_ANTIDEPRESSIVES.name: Antidepressants
TRT_ANTIDEPRESSIVES.description: Drugs used to treat depressive disorders. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Neurology dpt

TRT_ANTIDIARRHEALS.name: Antidiarrheals
TRT_ANTIDIARRHEALS.description: Drugs that stop diarrhea, relieve the symptoms, but don't eliminate the cause. They usually work by decreasing bowel movement. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / DID

TRT_ANTIMYCOTICS.name: Antimycotics
TRT_ANTIMYCOTICS.description: Drugs with an antifungal effect. Antimycotics are used to treat fungal infections caused by a wide range of fungal species. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt

TRT_ANTIFUNGAL_OINTMENT.name: Antifungal Ointment
TRT_ANTIFUNGAL_OINTMENT.description: Antifungal ointment with almost no side effects, providing long-term cure of fungal infections. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room

TRT_ANTIHEMORRHAGICS.name: Antihemorrhagics
TRT_ANTIHEMORRHAGICS.description: Drugs used to stop bleedings. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

TRT_ANTIHISTAMINES.name: Antihistamines
TRT_ANTIHISTAMINES.description: Allergy treating drugs, opposing the activity of histamine receptors, that are responsible for the symptoms of allergy. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt / Traumatology dpt

TRT_ANTIHYPERTENSIVES.name: Antihypertensive Medication
TRT_ANTIHYPERTENSIVES.description: Drugs used to treat hypertension - high blood pressure. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Cardiology dpt / Neurology dpt

TRT_ANTICHOLINERGICS.name: Anticholinergics

TRT_B-COMPLEX_SUPPLEMENT.name: B-Complex Supplement

TRT_BANDAGE.name: Bandage

TRT_BETA_BLOCKERS.name: Beta Blockers
TRT_BETA_BLOCKERS.description: Medication that is used to treat abnormal heart rhythm,high blood pressure, heart failure and tremor. \n\nRequired room: Any office / diagnostic unit at anz department except Orthopaedy dpt

TRT_BLOOD_TRANSFUSION.name: Blood Transfusion

TRT_CORTICOSTEROIDS.name: Corticosteroids

Office/Diagnostic unit / Regular ward / High dependency unit at Internal Medicine dpt

TRT_IRON_SUPPLEMENT.name: Iron Supplement

TRT_STOMACHICS.name: Stomachics

TRT_VITAMIN_SUPPLEMENTATION.name: Vitamin Supplementation

TRT_FRACTURE_SURGERY_HEAD_OT.name: Fracture Neurosurgery - Head
